I've purchased this product around spring '18 used it and it was amazing, talk about hair feeling soft as butter. I mean your fingers, comb, brush or whatever you use will go straight through your hair with ease. I left it in for about a couple hours since I've haven't had a protein treatment in years. But THAT'S how soft, silky smooth it was from root to tip after my first use.

From protein treatments I reviewed and talking to hairstylist I understood that I should use it 1x every 6 weeks but it's truly based off your hair needs. So I decided 1x a month was ok for mine at this point. I purchased and used again in July '18 with similar results but not as silky because I cut the time it set in my hair to a little over 30 mins. Mainly because I didnt want it that soft because my hair is fine but curly, coily and straighten it was like baby hair all over my head although my hair is bra strap length.

I purchased 2 more packs in Aug '18 and noticed the packaging was slightly different. Instead of saying "made with natural coconut oil and monoi" it now reads "made with FAIR TRADE coconut oil and monoi". I then compared them together and noticed it no longer says "Made with natural Vitamin E (Tocopherol)" above the ingredients list either. I continued to read the ingredients list and a couple things have been removed which are: Helianthus Annuus (Sunflower) seed oil and whats been added in its place is Tocopheryl Acetate, down the list these other ingredients have been simply added not replacing anything Leuconostoc/Raddish Root Ferment Filtrate, Dimethyl Stearamine.

I said that to say this, my hair personally loved the last ingredient list as it was, but this new list my hair is not okay with because it seems like it's more drying when applying on wet hair. Versus the last packages I used, they glided on and conditioned my hair along with giving it protein. So if I cant find the original ingredients and packaging I'm gonna have to discontinue supporting this product and brand. It was the only thing from palmers that I've used in years. And I told the world (everyone I know) how great it was, bought and gave some out but no more after this.

This product isn't sold in the US unfortunately, so it may take a little bit longer to ship. That said, it's definitely worth the wait. I have extremely oily skin, and this does a very good job at lessening it. When I don't use this, I have to use multiple oil-blotting sheets throughout the day. My usual routine is to use a cleanser that helps with the oil - something with salicylic acid, or lately Dermologica's Dermal Clay Cleanser, then use some witch hazel as an astringent/toner. After that I use a little bit of oil-free moisturizer, then once that's try, I put this under base on. I noticed immediately that it dries very fast. It doesn't make your skin dry, but I don't have to wait forever for it to soak in before I put on my makeup - it helps the moisturizer soak in faster too. My pores looked much smaller, and my skin felt very smooth. Like other reviewers have said, it made a very nice canvas for the makeup.
You may find yourself surprised at the size when you receive it - the canister is much smaller than you might expect, and it's metal. Depending on how much you use, you might want to go ahead and buy two, just because of how long it will take you to receive your next one. Another word of warning - you'll need a lot less than you think. When you press on the pump, you really only need to tap it; it will put out a bunch of mousse very quickly, and it's easy to get more than you need.
All in all, it's a really fantastic product, and despite the price tag it's really worth it.

ADVANCED CLEAN / DEEP CLEAN
This product is currently undergoing a branding/labeling transition that may be confusing for some. "Advanced Clean" used to be this products varietal name, but Colgate is now applying the "Advanced" labeling to most of the Colgate Total lineup. The new labeling reads "Colgate Total Advanced", and features a prominent gold shield. The variety is now called "Deep Clean"

PACKAGING
Love the stand-up cap, which allows it to be stored on its end -- and take up less space in the medicine cabinet.

TASTE & TEXTURE
The taste is unsurprisingly minty, enough to impart a clean sensation and some lasting freshness, but not so intense it burns or lingers beyond it's welcome. The texture is typical of 'paste' type dentfrices, however it is subtly 'grittier'.

INGREDIENTS & HEALTH CLAIMS
Ingredients are mostly standard fare for mass-market toothpastes, including cleaning agents and fluoride for cavity prevention. There are however a few important differences that make the Colgate Total Advanced range, and the Deep Clean variety in particular unique amongst toothpastes here in the states.

All Colgate Total toothpastes contain triclosan, a skin and mucous membrane-safe germ killing ingredient that you likely already use daily in the form of anti-bacterial soaps, lotions, and sanitizers. When added to toothpaste it helps kill undesirable oral bacteria (the kind that cause plaque, tartar, gingivitis and cavities). Studies have shown that it has no long term detrimental effect on desirable oral flora ('good germs'). This is the ingredient that is not found in any other (to my knowledge) toothpaste available in the states, and is what allows them to make claims about 12 hours of efficacy.

Unique to the Deep Clean variety is a modified blend of silica (included in virtually all toothpastes as a cleaning and low-abrasion polishing agent). Colgate Advanced Deep Clean contains more silica, and in a wider range of grit, from very fine and undetectable to larger and more noticeably coarse. In my experience, this makes for more effective cleaning, and leaves my teeth feeling polished and glassy-smooth. It also helps prevent and even dislodge some existing tartar buildup between dental visits.

SOFTSCRUB FOR YOUR TEETH
I use this as my daily toothpaste, and while it is slightly grittier than other toothpastes, it is not coarse to the point of harshness or outright abrasiveness. If you have ever tried Pearl Drops or other home tooth polishes, this product is similar in concept but much much gentler and perfectly suitable for everyday use.

IT'S FOR YOU IF...
- You want healthy mouth, teeth and gums with fewer 'bad' germs
- You want to keep tartar from reforming after a good dental cleaning
- You like the feeling of glassy smooth, deeply cleaned teeth

THINK TWICE IF...
- You are triclosan-phobic (I am not, clearly). Some people believe this additive isn't good for you (in toothpaste, handsoap, or anywhere else for that matter)
- You have especially sensitive teeth and gums -- you may want to explore gentler options. If you consider your sensitivity level to be normal to only somewhat sensitive, I think this product would be fine.

RECOMMENDED: Yes.

Tea Tree Oil, Argan Oil. Coconut oil. Those are the ingredients listed right on the front of the bottle. Anybody who is familiar with using various soaps and lotions knows, all three of these have very distinct scents. Tea Tree oil, you can practically FEEL working when you smell it!. On first use, I thought to myself that it looked and smelled like regular Dial hand soap. This immediately led me to the ingredients list. It contains none of the three advertised ingredients. See picture.

It's possible that the ingredients are referred to as "Vegetable Oils" or "Essence. But you'd think they would identify the ingredients that are identified on the front of the bottle. Every other product I have used with these ingredients actually shows that the ingredients are inside! You'll notice that the ingredients list is not shown here on the Amazon page. What this page does display is a pretty silly statement: "Help your body promote natural levels of Argan Oil, Tea Tree Oil, and Coconut Oil. That makes no sense, because obviously your body has zero natural levels of any of these.

I received this product at a discount in exchange for an honest fair review, and I must be honest! If the ingredients advertised on the label are inside, then they need to be listed with the ingredients! If they AREN'T inside, Then Keeva needs to find something else to call their soap, because it's not Argan/Tea Tree/Coconut!

I have been using Odaban for at least 5 years for excessive underarm perspiration. For most of my life, I had to wear carefully selected shirts and blouses to disguise perspiration stains that reached down to my waist. On excessively hot days, I held my arms out slightly so that the dampness did not touch my clothing, leading to muscular aches and pains. I actually turned down a job offer because it required wearing a blouse that would show dark stains. Teaching middle school students was quite a trial, since they noticed and commenedt on my excessive sweating.
The first four days, I applied Odaban every night. Then it began to work. I reduced the amount I used it to once a week without issue. At this point, I often go 2-3 weeks before I need re-application. It may feel at times like you are perspiring because it feels cooler on the affected area, but a touch test confirms that there is absolutely no moisture.
Perhaps the most wonderful effect is that I can wear whatever I want. Tight long-sleeved T-shirts were always a distant dream for me. Now I wear them in every color of the rainbow. Nobody knows my secret (but you).
Of late, I'm applying it around my hairline with similar success. In the California summers I often work outside in extreme heat and drip sweat in rivers under my hairline. The sweat gets into my hair and is hard to brush out when it dries. No more.
Had it not been for Odaban, it's likely I would have quit teaching. I wanted to hide myself away. Now I have the distinction of being considered a cool dresser by 8th grade students who regularly compliment this 64 year old lady about her hip clothing choices. And the little wardrobe I once had is now busting at the seams.
Odaban is a lifesaver.

And that doesn't only go for razors. This thing is the worst purchase of any kind I've ever made in
my entire life. Nearly $200 worth of junk.

I've used nothing but Norelco razors since I was 15 years old. That is well over 25 years now. I've
bought more than 10 different Norelcos over the years. I had not and would not even consider
buying any other brand of razor. Some were better than others, but none even begins to compare
to this piece of junk. The then 10 year old razor I got as a hand-me-down when I was 15 shaved
100 times better than this piece of crap ever did.

I've always bought the top of line Norelco whenever something new and "better" came out. This
piece of crap was no exception. Oh how great they made it look on the commercials all over the
TV. The wonder shaver of shavers, they claimed. Just read the company description here on this
page. It is all lies.

Now I'm not all haired up like a gorilla or anything. I have just normal thickness of hair and
growth. I usually shave every day and with every other Norelco I've ever owned I could get the
job done in less than 3 minutes. Using this hunk of junk it takes well over 15 minutes of rubbing,
scrubbing, and shoving this thing around trying to get it to work. I've tried stretching my skin so
tight that the skin on my neck was pulled up to my earlobe and it still doesn't cut the hair. I've
tried going back and forth, up and down, around in circles, clockwise, counter clockwise,
everything imaginable and no matter how you do it, this piece of junk just does not shave.

Even with brand new blades this razor misses about 1 in 10 hairs on my neck. And when I say it
misses them, I mean it completely misses them. They are not cut at all. It misses about twice as
many under the jaw line. And all of this is after 15+ minutes of shaving! So much for their line of
"even on the neck".

Oh and don't worry, no one will be able to miss that you look like you had a blindfolded 3 year
old give you 10 second shave. I can assure you that after nearly half an hour of ramming and
scrubbing this thing around on your neck, it will have a nice red glow for about 2 or 3 hours that
just screams "hey look at my 15 minute halfass shave". And I do not even have sensitive skin.

If you plan to use the pop up trimmer it is as big of a joke as the rest of the razor. It is about half
an inch wide and while it does work better than the rest of the razor it sounds like a wood chipper
when it is cutting. Think I'm joking? Just get one and try it. The first time I used it I actually
yanked it away from my neck. It didn't pinch or cut me but the sound of it made me think that it
had.

Oh and do not let me forget, you also get the pleasure of stopping and emptying the hair
chambers at least twice during each shave. If you fail to do this it cuts even worse (if such a thing
is possible) because this razor effectively does not have a place to store what hair it does manage
to cut. There are 3 individual hair "chambers" on the razor. One for each cutting head. They fill
up very quickly and then begin to puke the cut hair all over whatever you are standing over while
you are "shaving".

Now after you clean the bathroom sink (or wherever you were) of all the puked hair, you still get
to clean the razor. Don't worry, it is just as easy as running it under the faucet, right? That is what
they claim. Simply running it under the facet works for a few times but that does not get
everything out of the razor and it builds up over the course of a few days.

Now the geniuses that made this piece of junk cleverly decided to make the cutting head all one
piece. Unlike every other Norelco I've ever owned you can not take the cutters out of the combs
and clean them. They are made as all one piece. You cannot even take the cutter and comb out of
the head, everything is all molded together. All you can do is stick the cleaning brush that they
give you (which is as big a piece of junk as the razor) in behind the cutters and try to scrape out
what you can while spinning the cutter around and around. I quickly wore out the the cleaning
brush they supplied and started using a stiff bristle toothbrush instead. That doesn't work very
well either but it is much better than what they give you.

I've tried many different ways of cleaning the heads of this thing and the best way I came up with
was to use a container with a sealable lid and fill it halfway with very hot water. Spray some
cleaner on the head and put it in the container of water and shake it around as hard as you can for
a couple of minutes. I used foaming bathroom cleaner to clean it. Worked well. Might as well use
what you have and with this razor you are going to use a lot of that anyway to clean up all of the
puked hair.

Lastly, if while shaving you are unfortunate enough to get anywhere near to the hair on your head
with this wonder machine, it will suck it up like a Hoover from 3 inches away. Can't cut the hair
on your face and neck to save its life but it will grab hold of your hair from 3 inches away a suck
it into the cutters. Now with the other Norelcos I've had that was never too much of a problem.
You just take the cutter and comb apart and take the stray hair out, but remember this was
designed as a single piece so you do not get to do that. I've spent 5 minutes many times, fishing a
single head hair out of one of the cutters.

So in conclusion, just how bad is this razor?

After a year of all of the above I have just completely had it with the piece junk. And like I said, I've never
owned any other brand of razor, but I feel completely lied to, cheated, and ripped off by this razor
and Norelco. My solution: I have just ordered a Panasonic and I will never again buy anything
made by Norelco. I hope they choke on my money, they will never get another cent of it. EVER!

The aventurine/jade in the massage roller and gua sha differ in terms of coloration. However, it does not detract from the overall quality and attractiveness of the set. The presentation further lifts the value as a gifting option.
The gua sha has a nice thickness, and has a good degree of polishing. This creates a shiny and very smooth surface. The contour has smooth rounding, with clean lines. Inspection of the heart shaped gua sha found no defects or flaws. Happily, there are not scratches or nicks to be found on the aventurine gua sha.
The massage roller has features associated with premium level products. It has the metal collar/ring on the metal axle/arm where it meets the stone roller. This is a nice aesthetic touch.
An important aspect, which isn't a cosmetic affect, is the plastic grommet that acts as a bearing between the metal arm/axle and the stone rollers. This plastic piece facilitates a quiet and smooth rolling action; without this grommet, there's a good possibility of noticeable squeaking and a 'rough' rolling motion. Of equal import, the grommet protects the aventurine and the metal arm from direct contact, and avoids erosion of both parts.
The set matches the description and images in the product details. I appreciate that the company is upfront about the stone being aventurine (it is often called Autralian or Indian Jade, due to the appearance, but is actually part of the quartz family). Many companies use the jade classification to enhance the credentials of the product. It's not deceptive or misleading. Anyway, glad to see the company provide an honest account of the materials.
Attached are photos to show real world images, with a ruler for size reference.
Based on the quality and initial impressions, this massage roller and gua sha set earns a 4.5 - 4.75 star rating, with a high level recommendation.

I came to Amazon looking for a replacement comb for this trimmer. No fault of Remington, my trimmer is 3+ years old and has suffered more than one drop to the floor. The adjustable comb isn't made of titanium and you can expect to eventually break anything that is small and made of plastic.

A Remington rep saw my inquiry and asked me to contact them at the customer support email. I sent them an email and they called me the next day. After providing basic information, my rep said he would send me a replacement for free. The replacement arrived today ( 06/02/22 ) and not only did he send me the comb, he sent 2 other models just to make sure that I would have the right one for my trimmer. I don't know about you, but where I come from that's called "excellent customer service".

The CEO of Remington used to do a lot of commercials for the company and his famous tag line was, "I liked it so much, I bought the company"( ! ) He was an old school American entrepreneur who understood how valuable happy customers and word of mouth were to attaining and keeping success. It's good to see that his way of doing business lives on at Remington.

My trimmer is 3 years old. Remington was under ZERO obligation to do what they did for me. And yet, they did. If you have an issue with the product or ANY product, take the time to reach out to the company. Put the ball in THEIR court and give them a chance to make things right.

Thanks,

-J.

This is an excellent product. It can be very very messy though, so make sure when you do get it you get it in the tube with the needle nose applicator, and not the brush on type. I use this product in making seamless tape in hair extensions.  I apply it to the hair Wefting tape before I stick the hair weft to it, and then I put another line of it on of the other side and sandwich the hair in between the two strips of tape. Then just apply heat to the tape/glue using your flat iron. It only takes a few seconds. The heat activates this Glue to make an extremely strong seamless tape in extension that does not come apart. This product is absolutely ideal for that. You can use it to create taped in hair extensions that last for multiple installs. I have tried to use this product a few times to just Glue weft extensions directly to hair, but this does not work so well even though I believe that is specifically what this product is sold for. They usually slip and release after the first shampoo when you use that way; but for making seamless weft hair extensions for tape in application, there is not a better product out there. Feel free to contact me if you'd like to know how to make your own hair extensions.

I have struggled with chronic, reacurrent folliculitis on my legs (I have history of MRSA+/boils) for several years. This greatly helped keep my legs moiturized. IT was NOT strong enough by itself to keep pimples and boils from increasing or stopping ( I did try it alone) But works WONDERS when combined with ASAP silver gel or Activz silver gel. I use about very little of this cream or their similar Hand and Body Cream and mix it with the gel (use more then twice amount of gel then this... this is very creamy) I spread liberly on my legs and let it dry before putting pants on. In case anyone was wondering why I couldn't just use silver gel: because it does not moisturise... it dries-> my legs become very itchy-> I sometimes loose it and scratch-> the folliculitis gets worse/spreads. This and ABL Hand and Body lotion is the only thing I could successfully combine with the gel to keep my skin moisturised.. and I tried many things/oils. This, with silver gel is my "quick method" I found that has CLEARED my folliculitis and I have had been mostly clear legs for over 2 months now! If I ever get a pimple I apply Luguls Iodine only on it (and then silver gel with this cream on entire legs). Sometimes, for quicker results, I mix this cream, silver gel and essential oils of lavender and/or tea tree oil... but that was only if things started to look bad again. But I rarely have to do this as long as I apply this cream with silver gel in the morning and before bed and as long as I do not scratch my legs... which this cream pretty much solved the problem and my legs are rarely dry:)

This is a factory replacement part for several models of Braun shavers, so it fits perfectly, and it works just like the original part.

The bad news is the price. You'll hold it in your hand and say, "How could this thing cost $30!?" Well, it just does; if you want the factory part, you have to pay the factory price. There is no after-market replacement.

Do you even need to replace the cutter assembly? Probably so. Obviously, if you dent the original by fumbling your shaver, you'll need a replacement. However, even with perfect care, the original cutter wears out in about 18 months. If you pop the foil cover off of your shaver, you can see why: The blades run back and forth against the foil cover, shearing off the whiskers that have protruded into the tiny holes and slots. Eighteen months of friction does the damage.

So, if you want the Braun shaver-- which, by the way is excellent-- then you just have to buy the parts needed to do the periodic maintenance. Life is like that sometimes...

I've been used this specific shampoo only a couple of times as it was offered by vendor as a 'for review' item but we've been into Argan Oil shampoos for the past half year or so and we are very happy with the results. In fact our teenage daughter insists that whatever shampoo we purchase it contains the Argan Oil.

This shampoo provided a thorough, gentle cleaning of my hair and scalp and there is not discomfort or itchy feeling whatsoever after use which is a good thing. Our daughter likes it too but she observed that 'it smells funny'. I do agree that there is a strong, not necessarily unpleasant, smell for a brief few seconds while you are applying the shampoo on hair.

Additional facts worth sharing:

- It comes in a tube and a tube this size should probably last 2-3 months if used by a couple of people so, while the price is higher than what you'll typically see at a supermarket, it's not THAT high when spread over several months and it's worth the extra few dollars.
- While it's not stated anywhere, it seems to act as a conditioner. I would not use use a conditioner after this shampoo myself.
- For what is worth, the ingredients include: Aloe leaf extract, Argan oil, Xanthan gum and dozen of ingredients that are difficult to spell or pronounce but the maker promise that there are no petrochemical derivatives or parabens and that no animal testing was conducted.

The odds are I will continue to use this shampoo until I am made aware of a superior product. Until then, I am happy with it.
____________________________________________________

NOTE: If you are curious about Argan oil - apparently very good for both hair and skin - Wikipedia has a very good article.

The first time I purchased this, I loved it. It felt great, smelled good, and worked nicely to get away from regular deodorant/antiperspirant. I was really pleased with the product - never had a problem with staining, only a very minor reddening of the skin on one armpit during the first week of use and no further skin reactions or anything other than a pleasant experience. You don't have to use much to get good results. One little bottle seemed to last forever!

My second purchase didn't go so well. The first thing I noticed was that where the first jar had been perfectly smooth, the new one seemed more gritty or clumpy. I let it warm some and mixed it around to improve consistency, and things seemed OK until I was doing laundry and realized I had ruined two nice shirts with disgusting, oily stains - something that never happened the first time around. This just seems weird to me, because I would assume the second jar with its grittier, dryer composition would be less oily.

I reduced the amount I was using even further and made sure it had absorbed more fully before putting on a shirt, and things seemed better for the next week. Then my armpits started breaking out with a painful, blistering rash. Again, something I never experienced with the first jar.

I threw the second one away, and won't be purchasing this product again. The first experience was great, but the changed formula--or perhaps it didn't change and it's just inconsistent across batches--was awful. It took a couple of weeks for the rash this caused to go away, which was painful, embarrassing and inconvenient.

This is a quality IPL Hair Removal Device. I have used a few over the years and by far this is my favorite so far! I love how sleek and easy to hold it is. It's not a big and bulky unit that feels awkward. It's almost like holding a shaver for your legs. This IPL has an integrated skin tone sensor that scans your skin before every flash and automatically selects the most powerful treatment for your skintone. It also has 3 comfort modes, and an attachment for more precise areas of hair removal such as your lip area or T zone. The attachment really gives you a lot of freedom to get closer to areas you wouldn't normally be able to without worrying about zapping hair that you wanted to keep. I love that! I'm loving the results I'm seeing after just using this for a short period of time. Hair be gone! Hopefully I'll only need to use this as a touchup once every 4 weeks once I reach the results I'm happy with. I'm seeing less hair regrowth already! Definitely a wonderful IPL device!

I've been fighting the war of the beards since about 1952 when I was working at a TV shop where we sold Shick electric shavers so that's what I started with. Except during army basic training I have never used anything but electric. I bought a Norelco rotary at the PX once and promptly gifted it to another soldier as it always lost the battle against my beard. Many years later I was very happy using the shaver that caused Victor Kiam to purchase Remington and I still have several of those that use the now unavailable SP42 head. As it's replacement I have an MS2 that uses the SP69 head. That gives a nice close shave but it's gone from the sales world too. It is great for a close shave but no good if it's been over a day so the SP42 is used first to shorten the long stuff then the SP69 to finish the job. I was hoping this Wahl 7061-900 would be a good replacement for both those old shavers and it makes a good compromise. With a lot of effort it will plow through a 2 day growth and the final result is decent but not as close as the Remington with the SP69 head. I have not mastered the trimmer yet so I go back to either of my old Remingtons for trimming sideburns and to get a straight line on my mustache. If replacement heads ever become available for this shaver I'm hoping they may have solved the close shave problem but I think possibly there is no way to get a close shave and a 2 day beat down out of the same head. I guess the close shave requires a very thin screen while the 2 day requires larger holes that can't be put in a thin screen.

Overall, I'm fairly satisfied with my order of this spa set. The first thing I noticed is that everything smells fantastic! A strong coffee fragrance wafts from everything in this kit, and all three body products contain real coffee. The scrub was my personal favorite scent-wise; I find it smells just like a caramel lattee.

The body bar is nourishing and non-drying, but it is exfoliating due to the coffee grounds, so I wouldn't recommend it for everyday use. I do personally wish it was a tad larger, as it melts down quickly during typical usage.

The scrub is quite dense, as it doesn't contain added liquid. However, this means you get a lot for your money, as you only need to use a little bit on damp skin to get good exfoliation. I like the addition of almond and coconut oil, as it keeps it from being overly harsh, and makes it quite hydrating.

A little goes a long way with the bath soak. I love using it in a foot bath; It leaves my feet feeling nice and soft, and the aroma is relaxing and pleasant. It does dye the water a light brown due to the coffee, however.

I like the candle, but I'd consider it my least favorite out of this set. It has a nice burn time for its size, and it's in a beautiful black candle tin with a lid. However, I found the amount of scent it provided while burning to be underwhelming.

Overall, I really like this spa kit, and I think it'd be fantastic for any coffee lover. It's a little expensive at the current price, but if the candle was a little stronger, and the bath bar was a little larger, then this would be a five-star product.

Other reviewers have covered just about everything that I would have to say, so let me just add a "me, too" to what I consider to be the most important points.

1) Very, very comfortable shave, the most comfortable I've ever had. (I will qualify that by saying that this is the first electric shaver I've used in the shower, so as far as that type of shaving goes, my experience is limited.) It's not as close as a blade -- almost, but not quite.

2) It's very easy to clean, charges fast, and holds a charge for a pretty good length of time (probably about 45 minutes or so of shaving time).

3) The gel and lotion packs are a neat idea and are very convenient, but they are exceptionally pricey. They're about $1.25 each, and they last only about a week. I do use them when I travel for the convenience, but at home, I use regular shaving gel, and I encourage you to try the same. It saves a lot of money.

4) As with any new shaving technique, you will need to experiment a bit to find out what works at what doesn't as far as direction of motion, amount of pressure, and the like. They do say in the manual that you should give it three weeks, so if you don't like it at first, give it some time and try different shaving methods.

5) I've never actually dropped mine, so I can't confirm or deny whether it breaks as easily as others have said it does, but the shaver does look a little delicate, so being careful is probably prudent.

6) I don't use the trimmer, either, but I can see how having to change the attachment would be annoying, as others have reported.

All in all, I'm very pleased with the shaver. It makes shaving easy, convenient, and even fun -- so much so that, for the first time since I started shaving over twenty years ago, I'm actually shaving every day. For me, that says more about the shaver than anything else could.

I purchased this Wahl shaver to replace a seven year old Remington of similar design who's battery finally died. This Wahl shaver gives me as good of a shave as the Remingtons I've had in the past, and the battery life is impressive. I usually charge it once every two weeks, compared to once a week with my last Remington.

After three months of use, I did have a problem with my new Wahl - the reason for a four star rather than five star review. The finish on the gray plastic shaver head began to crinkle and flake off (see first picture). Since it was past the return period, I did not contact the Amazon seller but emailed Wahl directly and asked if they would replace the shave head under warranty. They quickly shipped me a free replacement shaver with all accessories in sealed retail packaging. The new version has a metallic chrome finish every place where the original was painted gray (see second picture). Note: the original and new version both have the same model number: 7061-900) . Great customer service from Wahl!

Just had my first shave with this. I've used two electric razors in my life. One was an already ancient one that my dad had, about 40 years ago. Ripped each of the few hairs in my face out one by one. Then an early Norelco 3-head circular job, about 30 years ago that either sucked or I didn't know how to use. Very even stubble was the result of that. Hence my refusal to try one until my memories have faded.

This review reflects one shave - not a long term review.

I'm impressed. It's slower than I expected, I half-thought I could activate an app on my phone or something and I would be shaved (I'm joking!). It's pretty darn close to a manual 5-blade fresh razor.

Here are the conditions under which I'm reporting:
- I'm 62, so my skin isn't exactly taut on my face.
- Not a heavy beard, I need to shave every day now, but not until I was about 35.
- This shave was with 2 days of stubble.
- I usually shave in the shower, using Braille to tell when a patch is smooth.

I used the razor dry. The manual said to charge the razor before first use, but it came out of the box showing 100% charge so I went for it. The manual also says not to apply too much pressure, so I was tentative at first. It helps to apply fair pressure while moving the razor SLOWLY through that fungus that seems to grow constantly on our faces.

Stretching my skin with other-handed fingers in most spots helped. I used a mirror just to watch the progress, but you can use your sonic sensors, AKA ears, to tell when the blades are and aren't removing stubble. I don't think a mirror is at all necessary, YMMV.

While I've been writing this, I've used Braille again to check on the usual trouble spots - I can just grab the mower and run it over the rough bits while I'm doing this. You could do this easily while driving somewhere - no risk of a small knick, and the aural attention that it takes doesn't really distract from driving.

I didn't notice when I ordered this razor that it came with an extra foil. Nice detail, since the foil is easy to damage being thin (as it should be). The kit comes with a carrying bag, which is nice to keep everything together while traveling. I would find a small rigid container for the extra foil if I were to travel with that - it could easily get bent in the carrying bag knocking around with all the bits. The foil is too long for the old canisters from film cameras, but a cut-off from a roll of Xmas paper would suffice.

The grip is comfortable and makes the shaving head easy to maneuver. The carrying bag contains everything in a small package - I'm a Cardinal (Archbishops and above don't shave) in the Church of Small Luggage and I will happily carry this instead of a couple of manual razors on any trip.

Finally (applause erupts from the crowd), very nearly close shave as a manual razor, a little slower than a manual razor, works very well dry (absolutely unlike a manual razor, though I've tried that also and it does sort of work).

Buy it. Try it.

So I’ve been following manscaped for a couple of years and have always been interested in their products, both on design, their specifications, and their advertising.

I was honestly pretty dissatisfied by both the Lawnmower and their nose tool. I’ll give my personal non biased pros and cons on the Lawnmower because that’s where I would like to discuss my experience.

Lawnmower Pros: the battery life is amazing, I haven’t charged mine in a month and it still has battery life. Also the led light is very bright and convenient. The design is very modern and sleek, easy to hold use, and it extremely quiet.

Cons: the blade is not as skin proof as advertised, and when wet the bottom guide tugs making
It not very maneuverable. Secondly I whole heartedly belive I was mislead by the amount leftover from using it. It leaves behind at least a 1/16th amount of hair that you would have to go over again with a razor due to the blade not being able to get close enough. Why advertise a product knowing it can’t shave baby smooth like advertised?

Overall, for the price it’s definitely a great buy, the pros do outweigh the cons to some extent. But In the end a razor is much more effective and less of a chance for nipping yourself. Until they come out with a stubble remover, I don’t think this would be a product you’d want if you prefer smooth and no stubble.

After very minimal and barely noticeable success with whitening toothpastes and gels, I finally decided to give Crest Premium Whitestrips a try. Previously, I had found the cost to be prohibitive, but the fact that $5 or $7 coupons are offered in our Sunday paper with some regularity made the price a bit easier to swallow.

Just like the commercials say, I definitely did begin to see some improvement after about three days. I would describe the effect as more of a lightening than a whitening per se: the darker yellow spots on my teeth began to fade and to become less apparent. Now, after using the product as prescribed for a week, the color of my teeth is clearly improved; I wouldn't say that my teeth are bright white, but they are definitely more of an ivory or a paler yellow color. According to the package insert, you can immediately begin a second course of treatment for even better whitening effects, but I haven't yet decided if I want to lay out another $30 in order to do this. Also, I should mention that the effect is not so dramatic thta anyone has noticed (UNLIKE in the commercials!).

Now for the caveats. First of all, I found these strips to be a bit difficult to use. It can be hard to remove the strips from the protective backing without them becoming mangled, and it is very easy to get the gel on your fingertips (which causes a temporary whitening effect). Also, the strips are not ideally shaped for your mouth, and I found that they covered my front eight teeth only. Finally, while the top strip adhered fairly well, I found that the bottom strip was more tricky and tended to slide around, especially if I moved my mouth at all. The only solution I found to this was to wear the strips while commuting to work, which luckily gave me a 30-minute period when I did not need to talk. Otherwise, it can be difficult to find a good time to wear the strips, as the insert information advises against wearing them immediately after you brush your teeth, while sleeping, and while you are eating/drinking.

Unlike other reviewers, I did not have a problem with the taste of the strips: I could not detect anything while wearing them, and after taking them off, I thought that the gooey paste left behind was virtually tasteless. I did experience some sensitivity in a lower tooth which I had capped over 20 years ago. Although this sensation went away in time, it was uncomforable enough to make me cut the lower strip to fit around this particular tooth.

In summary, I did see clear--although not miraculous--results from this product and I would be willing to try the strips again, especially if the price were to come down a bit. Ultimately, I would rate Crest Whitestrips three and a half stars, as there is definitely room for improvement.

This review is for people who are trying to decide on the Sonicare or a Braun Oral B (or ANY spin brush for that matter). My previous toothbrush was an Braun Oral B, and in my experience, the Sonicare is way superior.

First of all, the Oral B has a rotating head. Well, after several uses, I've noticed that the head starts getting loose and my lips and cheeks sometimes get pinched in the gap between the head and the neck of the brush. This causes me to have to change the brush even before it's due. Also, it seems that the Oral B allows a lot of water to enter into the metal parts that make the head spin. Therefore, while brushing, the water along with toothpaste seem to clean those parts which results in dirty water in your mouth! It's not pretty sight rinsing out your mouth and toothbrush and seeing grey water in the sink!

So after dealing with spin brushes for several years, I decided to give the Sonicare a try. Besides all the hassles of the spin type brushes, most importantly, the Sonicare cleans better. I was surprised at how well it performed since I thought the Oral B was a huge step above a manual brush. But the Sonicare makes my teeth feel even *cleaner* and I love how it makes my gums feel as well! When the Sonicare first came out in the market, I knew that there was no way the head would even fit in my small mouth (with all my wisdom teeth in tact!) so that's why I never considered it before. However, the current design of the brushes is fantastic. They are compact and angled and I have no problems reaching all my teeth. I have a feeling once you try the Sonicare, you'll never go back to any other toothbrush. It's really *that* great!

The 9199XL's Formula 1 racing heritage is evident from the moment its svelte yet muscular form rolls out of the box. It gleams under the lights, the low purr of its motor merely hinting at the roar of raw power that will soon follow. But make no mistake. This is no common wifebeater of a NASCAR shaver for the unshaved masses. It is a personal hygiene appliance of Formula 1 distinction.

While others have crooned over the razor's patented reflex action, or waxed at length about the personal comfort control settings, some still measure the worth of an electric razor by its ability to quickly and closely relieve one of facial hair. The 9199XX succeeds wildly, largely due its innovative carbon fiber construction. The use of this lightweight space age material allows one to deftly maneuver the razor about the face at breakneck speeds, making hairpin turns around lips and other such obstacles with the grace and dexterity of a true thoroughbred. The Certificate of Authenticity that accompanies this sleek, limited edition marvel (only 175 units were fabricated) speaks to its pedigree. It is a great personal comfort to know that no more than 174 other U.S. consumers will ever experience the victory of owning the Philips Norelco 9199XL Limited Edition Formula 1 SmartTouch-SL Men's Shaving System.

[[VIDEOID:95d38d1bfe64588ba1e91fd129d5363d]] This is a must have to every women out there especially those who often play with there hair and wants to have a different hair look from time to time. It is very easy and quick to use that will give you a classy look in no time at all. There is also no need for bobby pins and other helping tools to secure your hair and maintain the style through the day. All you have to do is tie up your hair then place your hair inside the cut of the sponge and pull it down until you reach the last quarter of your hair. I had a Remington wet/dry that finally got outdated and I thought to replace it. I did a lot of research and decided to try this Panasonic. I was really excited when it got here and the first time I used it I loved it and was all set to write a raving review. But I thought I should wait a couple weeks. I am very unhappy with it, as it turns out. I wish I had my Remington back (or got a new Remington). Yes, the Remington is a little louder. But this one is loud too - you can hear both of them from many rooms away. So really, that's not a big deal. I ended up getting another Remington and it is much better.

What got me is two things with this one:
1) The sides that hold the razor in place are taller than the razor area. So when you go to get a close shave, the sides of the unit scratch/scrape your leg. It is plastic, so no blood drawn or anything, but it doesn't feel good. Has no one else noticed this? I went to do a quick shave on my calves and ended up with stripes from the sides of the unit scraping my legs. And it didn't even get the little stubble. I didn't think the Remington was that fantastic, but it did better than this. Someone suggested to put the side extenders down. I do see the extenders (per the angry reply to this post), but they are down all the way. The won't go any farther down. So your angry post is not applicable. If I did not apply force to my leg with the razor, the length of the stubble it left was ridiculous. Again, maybe very long stubble is just the way it is, but not with my two Remingtons. They got much closer without having to apply force.

2) It vents out the cut hair once in a while all over your leg. You could be shaving along la di da and all of a sudden get a huge area of hair. I thought "I already went over that area?" but it was just the hair that got spit out from the razor. It is a little gross, I'm always wiping the cut hair off my leg. I've had two Remington's that did not do this. The Remington might have left the hair on the ground, or kept it in its head for me to clean later, but I prefer the Remington that didn't spit out puffs of hair on my legs incrementally. Again, the angry reply to this about including a vacuum is a bit silly.

Another disadvantage is that this one's charger plugs straight into the outlet, instead of having a cord. So I have to find an outlet to leave it in to charge that my kids won't get to. I like the cord better, so I can plug the cord in behind my dresser and have it sitting on top of my dresser in the little charger base. This one, you have to put the razor in the charger directly into the outlet. I could deal with this though if it didn't spit hair out all over my leg or leave scrapes down my leg. Yes, you could get an extension cord, so it's not that big of a deal. Just one more thing to add to a poor design IMO.

The first time I used this, I could barely get the polish to lift up. I used cuticle oil and a cuticle pusher and I wound up tearing up some of my natural nail. After doing some reading, I saw a suggestion to use a traditional basecoat under the gel peel off basecoat. I thought that it sounded silly, but I was willing to try it, as I wanted to have an easy way to take off my gel polish. I have been using Nailtiques Nail Protein Formula 2 as my basecoat because my nails are thin and then tend to peel. Then I put the gel peel off basecoat over the Nailtiques. After that I polish as usual with whatever color/brand of gel polish I have selected. I used an orange stick to assist with lifting and peeling off the polish when I want to remove it, Following this method has worked REALLY well for me and I have done it 4 or 5 times now. No natural nail lifting up! This also lasts a long longer than when I tried to use UNT. UNT lifted on my nails after one 1-2 days usually. This lasts *easily* 1 week+ for me. Capping the free edge properly is key. I rated it 4 stars because, IMO, I shouldn't have to use a traditional basecoat with the peel off basecoat.

UPDATE 12.6.16: I have found that using an orange stick to assist in lifting the gel polish is a KEY FACTOR in making sure that your natural nail does not lift. I figured this out last night. Additional tips for minimal damage: file down your nails prior to taking off the polish. Breaking the seal on the free edge and then washing your hands helps loosen things up, making it easier to lift. Happy peeling!

Color me PLEASED!! After having my nails done with gel at the salon, damage to my nails not to mention damage to my pocketbook as well, I have been on the hunt for the perfect polish for an at home manicure. The polish needed to be lush, vibrant, easy to apply, fast drying and resilient. I know, I know, asking too much??  Orly Color Amp'd fit the bill perfectly.  A nice palette of colors ranging from pastels to dark and rich. The polish feels somewhat silky, very easy to apply. The brush is amazing! It is contoured to the shape of a nature nail with a nice curve at the tip so you can get in close to the cuticle without a lot of maneuvering. It is a thicker brush which fans out nicely and allows the brush to hold enough polish to paint a nail without having to go back to the bottle. The directions suggest waiting 8 minutes to allow the polish to dry which I do between each coat. Once the clear coat (make sure you buy the Orly Color Amp'd topcoat!) dries, I am off and running. No nicks, smudges...nothing. Every couple of days I swipe a coat of the topcoat on and my manicure can last 10 days. And, I am not a princess; I am cooking, cleaning, gardening and this polish holds up. One IMPORTANT note: the first time I used it, no basecoat ( basecoat is in the polish itself), two coat of color and the topcoat. Next day....chips!! ARGH. I went to Orly's website and found the answer. Alcohol. Yep, swipe your nails with alcohol before you polish, even if you just used remover and washed your hands. USE ALCOHOL. Works like a charm! Now what are you going to do with the extra time you have from not going to the salon??

I ordered a set of 14 and a set of 16 inch in ash blonde. The “ash” did seem to wash pretty quickly but so did the tone in my hair so it still blended pretty well. These are able to be curled, but you can tell when you apply heat that they aren’t 100% human hair. I’ve had them in for almost 6 weeks now and I just lost my first sandwich from slippage. They’re still in good enough condition I could have them retaped and moved back up for another 4-6 weeks at least. These survived an obstacle course race, a week at the beach and sweat from 5 mile runs in 90 degree 90% humidity heat. They do come straight but once they get wet they turn wavy and you can get a great beachy head with very little effort. I only washed them 1-2 time per week and conditioned the ends liberally when I did. I used Maui moisture shampoo and conditioner which is silicon, paraben, and sulfate free (heal & hydrate with shea butter) and herbal essence dry shampoo on my roots between washes. Avoid using heat on your hair and make sure it’s dry before you go to sleep, and either braid or pineapple your hair for bed. For the price, you really can’t do better. I bought this for my mother after reading reviews online from patients receiving radiation. She started using it 1 week prior to radiation on her face. She is only in the 2nd week of her treatments so will update later regarding her completed treatment. Update: Mom is on Day 13 of her radiation to her jaw/face and is experiencing mucositis and redness to her face. We are still using Miaderm and she is experiencing very little inflammation to her skin (on the outside). FYI: I showed the radiation oncologist Miaderm and he looked at the ingredients list and said it has alcohol do not use it.I told him it was developed by radiation oncologist and Phase III clinical trials. And that it was not the type of drying alcohol. He would not even listen to me. He said only use aquaform (which is a petroleum based product-YUCK)otherwise "you are going to come and say my skin is drying and blame me b/c I said it was okay to use" He needs a refresher on chemistry - Yes the word "alcohol" is in the ingredients list but it is ceteryl alcohol different from the fermented alcohol that is drying. After leaving the clinic,  I pulled up the ingredient list for Aquaform and it had "lanolin alcohol" as an ingredient.. again it is a fatty conditioning "alcohol" not the drying kind-- urgh I wish I had known this to show him aquaform ingredient list!! Do not let the arrogance of doctors stop you from finding the best product for you.It is unfortunate that we can't always have doctors who will take the initiative to learn about a new product, research it, and then give us their opinion rather than just dismissing the patient.  I don't expect doctors to know the newest and best products on the market, but I do expect them to be open to learning about new products that may be helpful to their clients.

This is a really neat razor that has one possible issue, which I'll get to later.

The handle is big and heavy, and its unusual shape is very stylish. It gives a nice shave, too. If you have a small face or tight features, the large head might be a bit tricky to use in some areas, but that hasn't been a major issue. I usually use an old-school double-edge razor, not a flexing cartridge razor, so the fixed head angle isn't an issue for me. If you're used to a razor with a movable head, this one might take some getting used to.

The included shaving foam smells good and works well. You don't get a lot, but you don't have to use a large amount of it.

The "stand" is just a heavy metal cylinder with a round hole in the top and a pad on the bottom. The hole is wider than the razor handle, and just deep enough that the razor doesn't topple out. It's kind of weird, like the stand was designed for something else, but I guess it works.

I'm not sure why they call this package a "travelling razor kit." It comes in a large gift box, and doesn't include any sort of travel case or bag. Plus, it's the biggest, heaviest razor I've ever seen (aside from folding straight razors). I wouldn't even think of taking this thing on a trip.

Despite all the good things about this razor, there's one major red flag: At the moment, replacement blades aren't listed on Amazon or on the Suprent website. The package includes two cartridges and a card to get a third for free, but there's no guarantee that more will be available when you need 'em. While the included cartridges are definitely good for several shaves apiece, if that's all you get, you'll soon be left with a very expensive paperweight. I rated the product based on my use of it, but recommend that you verify the availability of replacement cartridges before buying.

I have naturally curly, kinky hair. I have seen these hair sponges in plenty of YouTube hair tutorials. I thought that this would be a nice tool to have for the summer when I am able to rock my fro a bit more. The way this sponge works is that you lightly press it onto damp hair and move the sponge in small circles all around your hair. The end result is a bunch of neat little twists all over your head. It's really cute.

This package arrived on time. I had a bit of trouble opening the package, but that's okay. The sponge itself is black, and has a topper that is a bit stiffer and is blue. The blue part of the sponge goes in your hand and the black part of the sponge goes towards your hair. There are small-medium sized holes in the sponge. These holes help to make the twists in the hair as the sponge is moved around the head. I was excited to try this sponge, but was a bit disappointed. I think my natural hair is too long for it to work like I wanted it too. I have armpit length hair and it is difficult to get my hair to stand on end. I tried this sponge on my brother, who has a much shorter fro, and it worked for him. He was able to get a lot of definition in his hair. I think that this product works very well on shorter natural hair rather than long hair. I will give it another try soon. I am determined to make it work for me!

This Spa-Da Immunity Support Soap is one of the more interesting soaps I have tried. You get a pack of 6 small shield shaped soaps in a resealable plastic bag. The soaps are about 2.75 inches in length.

The scent is strong when you open the bag and especially in the shower. Some of the oils and ingredients included are cloves, cinnamon, oregano, peppermint, parsley, eucalyptus, and rosemary. If you don’t like the smell of cloves, then do not order this because that is the most predominant smell in my opinion. I like it though- it has a nice spicy kick.

The soap definitely feels different than I expected. It doesn’t feel really smooth like many soaps. When dry, they were slightly powdery when I ran my finger across. They are slightly gritty feeling which I noticed more once the soap became wet in the shower. This could be nice if you like a slight scrubby feeling. I typically rub it onto my mesh bath sponge or silicone sponge so I don’t really care about the grittiness either way. I found that it lathers and suds up very well with both my mesh and silicone sponges.

I just started using, so I’m not sure how many showers I’ll be able to get out of each bar. Honestly, I thought they might disintegrate and get used up fast simply based on how powdery they felt when dry. I don’t think it will last as long as some other soaps, but I was still surprised that I could somewhat see the outline of where the shallow cross was imprinted on the soap even after my first shower-and I did intentionally scrub my sponges all over that side pretty vigorously. I took a picture of what the soap looked like after one shower compared to unused bars.

Anyway, my skin feels very clean, perhaps even a little moisturized after using and I haven’t experienced any irritation to my sensitive skin (I have eczema).

I like that this soap is natural and free from harmful chemicals. I also love that it is made in the USA-Ohio specifically, which is where I am from.

I’ll be sure to update my review if any issues arise like if I find that the bars do in fact get used far too quickly. I hope my review was helpful in some way, and if so, I would love to know- thanks!!!

Not a big a deal, but a little annoying. Once I got the lash on, I couldn’t believe how uncomfortable they were. They were as close to the lash line as I could get. I removed and readjusted the lash and still super irritating. When I removed the lash however, it had taken off the liner where the magnets had been. Ok... so now the lash has that nasty eyeliner all over it... I decided to give it another try, but this time I used one of my eyeliner brushes to apply the liner to my other eye. I also tried the largest lash since the one I just applied had liner all over the magnets. The liner was still awful but the lash was a little better except for in one spot where there was extra glue on the lash itself which again made the lash very uncomfortable to wear. Also, the lash kept wanting to lift away from my natural lash line. In the pic you can kind of see this in the corners of my eye. Better fit though although not super comfortable. I still didn’t think I’d be able to wear all day or even for an hour without wanting to rip it off. Anyway, I removed that lash and a magnet came off with it. Again, I followed the directions exactly. What a mess. What a waste.

I've had these for almost a year but I haven't used them that much--there are a select few designs that I use regularly, but the majority have remained untouched. Here's why:
-A lot of my plates came damaged; maybe I'm just the unlucky one in a thousand, but many had imperfections in the metal that ruined the images. Other than that, the undamaged plates worked decently. You might run the risk of having damaged plates like me, but I'm guessing this was just my bad luck. Can't really blame the company since I never made the effort to return them, and it's been waaay past 30 days (at the time I was just happy I got them).
-I liked that there were themed and matching stamps, but to be honest I just don't like a lot of the images enough to actually use them. There is an unnecessary amount of tribal/swirly images, and what were they thinking with the ambiguous pair of chili peppers? Take a good look at the individual images and take that into account more than the sheer number of plates. Will you actually use them?
-Unless you have child's hands, the full-nail plates are waay too small--it's most noticeable on the thumbs and middle fingers, but they can barely fit over any of my nails (in terms of width, not length--I don't have crazy-long nails). To avoid awkward-looking nails, I haven't used many of the full nail stamps. Images like the polka-dots are easy to align and restamp, but others not so much.
-They come off a little blurrier than Konads, but nothing too horrible
-Some of the images are nice in theory, but don't show up that well once stamped. For example, there's a cute lace french tip that I just couldn't get to work because it was impossible to pick up the full image.

On the plus side:
-they're cheap, and most of the images are pretty crisp
-A lot of the full-nail designs have matching french tips
-The french tips will definitely (probably) fit over all your nails, including thumbs.
-I like the themed plates--for example, there's a Christmas one and a Halloween one.
-I've heard complaints that these plates were too sharp, but I don't see how you could accidentally hurt yourself with them--maybe they've updated their plate design?

Although I haven't used many of the images, those I do use make the bundle worth it. The total price for all 21 image plates--damaged and unattractive though some may be--is the price of 3 Konad stamps. It's definitely worth the price, but I can't guarantee amazing quality.

I am in shock right now! I am 39 years old and have had a vertical wrinkle between my eyes since I was 32. I used all kinds of wrinkle creams, but it kept getting deeper and longer. When I would look in the mirror, it was the first thing I noticed.

I seriously thought about Botox, but then I read somewhere that you can use hair setting tape on your wrinkles over night when you sleep. Last night was the first time I used it, and I am amazed. I simply tore off a piece that was a little longer than the wrinkle, and laid it right on top. It didn't bother me at all.

The wrinkle is barely visible now. Of course, I know I will need to continue keeping the tape on it over night, but man is it worth it!

I'm guessing since I sleep on my side, that my face "squishes" when I sleep causing the vertical wrinkle. The tape probably works by keeping my skin in place while I'm sleeping.

First and foremost, the brushes I received are marked "SOFT" not "Extra Soft" as product title says.  I received eight (8) brushes in two sealed packaged of four (4) each.  I actually like the fact that the OEM Oral-B brushes are all in individually sealed packaging when you buy them in bulk.  I just think the individual packaging lends itself to the brushes remaining sterile until they are ready to be used.  I did notice some difference in the overall quality of the brush heads in comparison to the OEM heads.  The description refers to flossing action bristles; but the OEM Oral-B actually have several rubber flossing bristles amongst the other bristles.  These replacement brushes do not have the specialized flossing bristles.  But, all in all, these brushes seem to do a good job of cleaning my teeth at a FRACTION of the OEM cost.  They fit my Oral-B toothbrush body well, they stay in place, and they agitate properly.  Although the quality is not identical, these brush heads are a VERY GOOD option for a budget-conscious household.

In my younger days, I used to model, so I became an old hand at contouring, which seems to have been revived a la Kim Kardashian. One of my favorite brushes from then was a flat contouring brush that was angled. I absolutely adored it for adding the right shadow under my cheekbones to make them stand out.

Since the contouring revival, I decided to give this a go again, but I couldn't find a reasonably priced brush that did what I wanted it to do (I refuse to spend a fortune on a brush for this, mostly because I do not see contouring lasting very long, but that's another review.) The HDSL brush came closest to what I was looking for: dense, flat and with a good handle.

It kind of looks like a paint brush, but the hair is VERY nice. Very soft and dense, and a miracle for something that's less than six bucks. My contouring skills are such that I know not to buy an entire palette of colors that are way too orangey or warm. I use Kevyn Aucoin's contour powder which is way pricey (you can find something similar in color in Makeup Forever's eyeshadow line), but the Aucoin contour powder color was absolutely perfect.

As I'm sure you know, contour is meant to mimic a shadow. It is NOT a bronzer (you cannot contour with anything with the slightest bit of shine); too orange and you look ridiculous. The color is a light matte taupe with zero hint of red in it. It should NOT be too dark, unless you are dark complected. Think of it as being two shades darker than your skin color, the way a shadow is cast on the hollows or low areas of your face.

This brush picked up the powder like a champ and deposited it nicely. Does a great job fading the contour color out so it looks like a shadow and not like I have a swath of dirt on my face. My only hesitation with this so far is that I wish it was angled, but no matter. It's not that hard to use in such a way (well, what do you want for less than six bucks?)

I'd definitely recommend this. You won't get anything better at anywhere near the price.

These bath bombs exploded with aroma when I opened the box. Each bath bomb is individually wrapped in tissue paper and has a sticker describing the color and the scent. After the sticker and tissue paper are removed, there is a plastic barrier to protect the bath bomb from getting wet before it is supposed to. They are heavily perfumed. The tissue paper and stickers make for a nice display in a bathroom bowl until they are used.

The ingredients used are all natural and safe. Baking soda, citric acid, Shea butter, coconut oil, sea butter, olive oil, fragrance, and plant extract. These are all items we have in our household. When my wife has made her own bath bombs in the past she used many of the same ingredients. I like that there are no unknowns on the ingredient list. I also like that there are no dyes for the safety of my white tub but also for my children’s skin.

I have found bath bombs to be an awesome motivator to get our children to take baths. While bubbles are fun, bath bombs seem to have a longer lasting effect. The bath bombs have an instant fizzy effect when they meet water. The colors ooze out and the aroma fills the tub. My smaller closed in tub area has a more intense scent than my more open tub. These bombs are fragrant and have long lasting fizzy effects in the tub. The kids are happy and like taking bath bomb baths so I am pleased. Knowing they are made of safe ingredients leaves me feeling comfortable using them with the kids.

I've been using Norelco shavers for past 18 years, so I've always liked their product. Before I bought this item, I had an old (12 years old) Norelco that I wanted to replace. My brother had bought the 9160XL shaver a while ago and after I checked it out, I thought it looked nice and was much quieter than the older model that I had. I didn't use his to see how well it would shave, so I decided to take a chance and buy it based on his recommendation. However, I was skeptical about how good it would shave because it was too quiet. After I bought and received the shaver, my very first shave was simply great. I can't recall the last shave I had that made my face this smooth. The shave is so close that I don't get a "5 o'clock" shadow until the next day! Another very important thing is that after shaving, I don't get a burning/irritating sensation with this shaver. I don't have sensitive skin but my old Norelco would sometimes irritate my skin.
I also liked the face-contouring elements that they have added to this shaver. I don't have to fight and press the shaver as hard on my face to get the blades to touch my skin.
Cleaning the 9160XL is breeze; just rinse with water and let dry. Battery life is great too. I have used this for 2 months (over 30 shaves) and it still has 1/3 of its charge left!
I would strongly recommend this shaver.

I love this product so much! Once you catch the hang of the steps to take to get the best results you will be in love also. For the best results these are the steps that I took:
1. Shape nails and clean throughly, DO NOT APPLY OILS OR LOTIONS
2. Apply gel base and cure for 30 secs
3. Apply gel color and cure for 30-60 secs
4. Apply thin coat of no wipe gel top coat and cure for 30 secs
5. Apply powder using eye shadow brush and rub in completely over entire nail...apply a little pressure to make sure powder is sticking
6. Brush off any excess powder before applying a clear top coat and cure for 30-60 secs
7. After making sure that the nails are completely cured wipe off using a no lint cotton pad and alcohol

I love this mirror. I have used it for nearly three years and have been happy with it. When I first purchased it, i was planning to use the battery feature as i didn’t want another appliance that needed to be plugged in on my bathroom counter. But that battery feature is weak, the batteries run low quickly and the light is not as bright on battery power. Totally worth plugging it in for the brightness. The design flaw is in the rotation from magnified to unmagnified. You can rotate the mirror any direction and number of times you want. So, unless you are disciplined enough to only rotate the mirror 90 degrees one way and then back the opposite direction, you are effectively twisting the wires. We all know that if you twist wires enough they become faulty. Had the design included a stop on the rotation so that you could only rotate it one way and then back the other, it would reduce the chances of the wires twisting too much. Over the years I am sure I have twisted them a few too many times now so the light will sometimes flicker or become dim because those wires are weak and losing connection. One simple mechanism could have improved the longevity of this mirror.

I think we all have days where we're looking for something quick and easy when it comes to makeup.  Anything to get us out the door looking somewhat put together with the least amount of time involved is the goal.  If you're looking for a quick eyeshadow look that takes next to no time these are one answer.  Cream eyeshadow on one end and a brush on the other.  The brush is surprisingly usable, but since I've got older eyes (which means older skin that needs to be babied) I just use one of mine.  They go on pretty smooth and creamy.  Not a ton of pigment on the first pass, but it's very buildable.  The swatches on my arm are 2 passes of each color.  They also blend out really well.  I put a couple passes on my lower lid and blend it up into my crease and I'm good to go.  There is a bit of shimmer in this one, but it's not overwhelming.  Just a bit of sparkle that looks good even on aging eyes.  They make it through the whole day, but I did notice some creasing and breakdown after about 6-8 hours.  Not a lot, just enough to be noticeable.  Overall these were pretty impressive and I think they'd also be great for someone who was just getting into makeup too.

For the price, this is an excellent basic electric toothbrush and flosser. The two pieces lack the fancy features of more expensive pieces, but my dentist says all those extra features are not worth the money. The toothbrush works great and the only feature my dentist recommends is the 2 minute timer to make sure you give yourself enough time to brush all your teeth. I've always used string floss in the past, but this water flosser seems to work just as well most of the time. Sometimes there is some food stuck in my teeth that I still have to use the string for.

As I said, features are minimal. There is 1 charger for both devices, so you can't use the charger as a stand for both items. Instead of all the cardboard in the box, why not include a cheap plastic tray or stand? There also is no travel case, just a little cover for the toothbrush head. I would really prefer a full length hard case to be included instead of having to buy an aftermarket case that doesn't fit well. Also, you only get one tip each for both the flosser and the toothbrush and there is nowhere to easily store extras. This kit is probably designed for single people only, not couples or families.

I watched many videos in preparation of arrival of this kit and was excited to try it. It's a really nice product to work with for a beginner. I haven't been to a nail salon in decades and just have natural nails. Sometimes they grow out long and beautiful but too often they break and are uneven lengths. That's particularly why I wanted a polygel product to allow my natural nails to grow and use this as an
overlay strengthener. I think it will work well for that. If you follow the directions and watch a few videos, prep the nails and wipe off the sticky layer, I feel this is a very good product and was lots of fun to play with on my current shorter nails. With practice, I hope to get better at application. I bought the glow in the dark set and I wasn't crazy about the opacity, so I painted over them. The reason I dropped a start, though, was the nail forms. The deap curve of the forms don't fit my nails very well and I was very glad I'd also ordered nail clips. Without them, there's no way they would have turned out looking decent. It made it difficult to squish out the extra and clean up my cuticles before curing. Cleaning up the under side of the nail to give it a proper shape underneath was a little bit of a challenge because I didn't notice I'd gotten really thin on a couple edges. One pinky nail bubbled between the gel and form because the clip wasn't firm enough on my pinky. These I had to patch. Some of the patches worked, one did not (right hand pointer). I have ordered the paper forms to use in the future and hope they will work better for me. All around I'm very happy with the glow in the dark kit and the one I tried, really does glow in the dark! Very cool and I think my granddaughters will love it! I only applied them yesterday and I'm confident in my nail prep and use of base coat. If I don't get decent wear out of them, because I only applied yesterday, I'll come back and update.

This is a tricky review to write. When I received the product, my initial reaction was, "What is this?" The photo on this item's page is very misleading. The product is not a dark creamy brown looking thing. It's basically pale brown colored sugar crystals. That's it. I contacted the seller with a photos (see photos) and said there seems to be something missing. They assured me "As for the packaging, thanks for letting me know, we will have it modified accordingly this week." I'm not sure what that means, but besides the photo being completely different, the description indicates it's 8.8 oz and it's an organic brown sugar. The ingredients don't indicate "organic" at all and the label on the jar states it's 7 oz. So it's not the product I thought I was purchasing. I'm bothered that the photo was SO different than the product I received. I honestly thought the cream or oil must have evaporated or something.

That said, my testing of the product has been odd and I'm torn about an accurate star rating. The first day, I wet my arm and took a scoop of the crystals and rubbed them over my arm. All of the crystals fell off into the sink within seconds and other than being wet, my arm was exactly the same. I was thinking that even one star was too generous for a useless product. Today, I gave it another try. I scooped the crystals up with my fingers instead of the plastic scoop. I did my best to keep them on my arm this time. My arm turned pretty pink during this process and I really didn't know what to expect. I was only able to do this for about 40 seconds before I lost the last of the crystals. I rinsed my arm and was impressed by how soft my arm felt. However, I know you can take salt and product a similar softness by using some oil along with it. So I don't know....it softens the skin but at the rate it's been disappearing on me, the product won't last long and there's really nothing extraordinary about it.

Note: I was able to purchase this item, at a discount, for my honest review.

A few things here …

LES ENFANTS is French for CHILDREN

It doesn’t refer to infants or toddlers. For those who don’t know, a quick note …

Newborn – birth to 4 weeks
Infants – 4 weeks to 1 year
Toddler – 1-3
Preschooler – 3-5
Child – 6-13

That said …

It does state NOT TO SPRAY IT ON A BABY’S SKIN. “Applying gently on clothes” is the preferred method. Though to be honest, I’d say this is more for ages 6+

While baby cologne isn’t something I would use on a baby, it could be better for adults who want a light scent and not overwhelmed by a perfume.

For almost $30, this is an 8.5 fl oz spray. Compared to some perfumes and colognes, this is moderately priced.

Smell? It isn’t the greatest. I do like it as a light, after-bathing scent. It seems more suited to a room freshener than a perfume. But, it isn’t the WORST perfume/scent I’ve smelled.

The generous size means it will last a while.

Would I get it again? No. Nor would I recommend it either.

I'm not sure if this just wasn't product tested with the shower actually on or if my product is defective. "suction cup bottom grips to any floor." The suction cups are a great idea for a shower product, but do they work on this one? ABSOLUTELY NOT. I don't know whose idea it was, but I would like to see someone actually cup this thing in the shower, put some soap in there, turn the water on and use it without having to superglue it to the floor.

It absolutely does not stay in one place no matter how light you try to get your foot in there. And I don't know why the pumice stone is there at the heel because you're lucky if you even get your arch in before it starts sliding away. I literally almost fell and broke my ankle the first time I tried to use this because I went into it thinking the suction cups would work. So just be warned, you need something sturdy to hold on to while using this product.

Another problem is the over-the-foot plastic. Why plastic?? If i'm not water skating through the shower on this thing and actually get my foot in all the way, that plastic hits the top of your foot and it's painful. I get the whole car wash appeal it looks like they're going for but that plastic needs to be made out of a softer material.

Since i'm assuming the paying customers are the only product testers in this case, I found it easiest to use one foot to hold the product down by stepping on the pumice stone and one foot doing a combined up/down left/right motion inside the "foot wash" while holding onto the shower rod with both hands. This did crack my pumice stone after 2 uses but at least it wasn't my back.

So as far as likelihood I would recommend this to a friend to use for its intended purpose, I would have to take a hard pass.
