I have extremely sensitive and dry skin.  I need moisture constantly but many of the drug store options either don't really absorb into my skin and leave breakouts which cause all sorts of other issues, or the product itself will cause inflammation.  When I was asked to try this product for my opinion I was hesitant because it can take a moment to destroy my skin and weeks to repair it.  I decided to try it out on my hands first.  I placed the smooth scentless cream on my left hand and none on my right.  It was light to touch but needed to be massaged in.  I only used about the size of a new pencil eraser and that was more than I needed.  After a couple of hours I noticed that my left hand still looked like I had applied lotion which was a nice change.  By the end of the day, the top of my hand and the skin at the base of my thumb were still looking good.  This was pretty amazing since I must have washed my hands a dozen or more times.  I decided to go with the face today.  Again I used about the size of a new pencil eraser and that covered my whole face.  It felt good and I did not feel like I needed to add any oil to my cheeks on top of it.  Its been a good 6 hours and they still feel like they did when I first applied the cream.  Also, no redness - that is a HUGE plus.  I am really happy I took the chance to test this product.  I like that it works well and that I can give it an honest good review.

After being scolded by the dentist one-too-many times that I wasn't brushing in circular motions and that I haven't been giving my wisdom teeth enough attention, I recently decided to jump onto the electric toothbrush bandwagon, but at the same time, I had no desire to spend half a truck payment on one with "sonic" cleaning or five-way floss/torture heads. Over the past month, I've found that this Oral-B Vitality, which is actually manufactured by Braun and bears a small Braun logo, cannot be beat for the money! It does just what I need it to, has a nifty compact recharging station with no exposed metal contacts, and my mouth hasn't ever felt cleaner.

The only two very minor cons that I think people should be aware of are related to battery life and the two-minute timer. It's probably true that the battery will last 20 minutes between charges, but I've found in daily use that if I don't hang it back on the recharging station after every other use, the bristles spin a bit slower than they do when the battery is fully charged. Not a huge deal, but my time is precious in the morning and I want maximum RPMs, so I prefer not to completely empty the battery beyond the manufacturer's recommendation to do so periodically. Also, I can see the two-minute timer being great for those who have a bad habit of not brushing long enough, but I find personally that I typically brush for about 2-1/2 minutes, even if I'm trying to rush, so it would be nice to have a way to turn the two-minute timer off. The timer isn't a light or a beep, but rather the toothbrush "revs" its engine down and back up a few times once two minutes have elapsed, which makes brushing ineffective over the course of those one to two seconds of revving and thus again wastes precious seconds. Again, not a huge deal, but a way to turn it off would be nice. Otherwise, this toothbrush can't be beat!

Wow. Super-easy to use...I just combed it into a tween's blonde hair (no bleaching). Gave the colour great dimension with the various shades of natural blonde. Would be hard to use on dark hair without bleaching.
I left it on for about an hour, unwrapped. I cannot figure out how you'd blow-dry hair that's IN a shower cap as you do it (part of the instructions), so I just removed the cap and did the blowdry while gently combing through the hair with a comb (until dry). Then a rinse - this is where all the blue looks like it's coming out. Use cooler water.

We re-dyed every 7 days, and the colour stayed vibrant.

Since then, it's been about two months and the electric blue went to pale cotton-candy blue, then to teal, now it's the colour that blonde hair goes with too much pool chlorine in the summer (sort of a washed-out green). I've heard that you can get more colour out by using a "clarifying" shampoo, but the tween seems happy with the transformation.

No real mess, but the bath does have a faint blue tinge to the bottom from the rinsing. It seems to scrub out a little more with each regular cleaning.

My daughter was looking for inexpensive brushes to replace her old ones. They arrived in a multi chrome (purple to blue shift) fake leather, which has 10 slots in a few sizes. It has two metal clasps that keep it closed, and she hasn't had any problems with it locking or opening randomly. The brushes themselves are decent quality, but GREAT for the price. The ‘gems’ are little plastic diamond shaped pieces in two colors (depending on the color you chose), and they are small enough so they aren’t uncomfortable when you are holding them and they provide a better grip for accurate use. The one problem she has with the diamonds is that they are held onto the brush with what my daughter could only describe as a piece of double sided tape. There hasn’t been an issue with the diamonds falling out, but I surprised if they eventually did. The rose gold metal on either end is a nice touch and it doesn’t clash. The brush ends are quite dense (depending on what brush you’re using) so it picks up product well and doesn’t suck up too much foundation and concealer. (And the extra brush with the spoolie and think brush for lining is a nice touch.)

Overall, I’d say it’s a great product for the price.

I learned some things when I was looking for hair cutting shears. Mostly that you can pay around $200 for the best ones,used by professionals,and,that you need very sharp scissors or shears to cut or trim hair.

I've been trimming and shaping my own hair for a long time and am usually happy with the results,considering too many times,when I would go to a salon to have it cut or shaped or trimmed,they did a job I was almost always disappointed with. I decided a long time ago,if someone was going to screw up my hair,it might as well be me. At least I have a good idea on when to stop cutting it and I actually care what the final result looks like.

I also have fine,wavy hair. Not the easiest to cut.

I have noticed,and maybe it was from using whatever scissors were around at the time,that sometimes the ends do look a bit frazzled;and in researching shears,I found out that unless the blades are very sharp,the ends can break,or result in split ends. I don't have split ends and never noticed them,except for a long,long time ago when I was a teenager;but,I have used these once to do some light trimming and shaping;with wavy hair you are always shaping,cutting with the wave and not cutting against the wave. And,these shears have produced a nice result. And I don't see the somewhat frazzled or lightningbolt effect at the ends of my hair,that I sometimes get.

I've used these just once,I only trim my hair to maintain healthy looking ends,and I did it a few weeks ago,and I like these and I'm glad I looked for something better than "any old scissors" that I have on hand.

Unlike others, my motivation for going with electric shaving was based on medicine. That is, I am so many different blood thinners that, no matter what shaving system I use, I managed to get at least a small cut or two and these would bleed and bleed, etc. So, it occurred to me to try an electric razor. I read the generally excellent reviews posted at this site--thanks to all of you guys for your input! I decided on this middle of the line Norelco. It's a couple of models from the bottom; the bottom one is cord only, the next one is cordless only, and then comes this model. I spent some time at the store looking over the different blade systems; even Norelco alone has several going. I knew I didn't want that squishy mess like the most expensive, and I was reluctant to sink a small fortune into something I might not like. What decided most of all for me to get this 'Reflex Plus' system was that it has a whisker collection chamber so they don't go all over the place. I think the model(s) above this have lithium ion batteries, whereas this model still has NiCads--which I hate. If I traveled more than I do, I'd spring for the model above for about $10 more. NiCads are slow to charge and are generally crummy; I've been using them in photo equipment for 30 years. This particular razor can be used just as easily plugged into the wall, which I like. I also like the fact that it doesn't require a separate charging stand, you just plug the cord into the bottom.

How did it shave? Great for me. I'm blond with a fairly soft beard and it plowed through about 3 days' worth of beard, making a chopping sound the whole time. I felt an occasional 'pull' but then this was fairly long beard. Once the long hair was gone I found it quite smooth to shave with. I bought some 'Lectric Shave' at the store at the same time I bought the razor but I didn't use it. Having fairly light-colored hair on my face, I never have the Fred Flintstone look regardless. I found, though, that the razor certainly got my beard acceptably close. I can still feel hair on my face, but not to an unacceptable degree. I have usually used a German Mercury double-edge razor with Wilkinson Sword blades and this doesn't shave that close. It might do better or different with another's beard. As for me, I'm delighted, and it didn't cut me at all. I'm in my forties, so don't have any deep wrinkles, etc., to cut through. That might be different.

Unlike some of the other razors I looked at, this model is light to hold and not bulky. Some of the other, more expensive models seemed both huge and heavy. I wasn't willing to mess with that. Having only used this a couple of weeks, I can't speak to the long-term durability. Seems appropriate to the price, though. I don't think it'll cost me particularly more than blades and shave gel in the long run. It is very quiet. I am looking forward to many more pleasant shaves!

These come in a nice little box. They are vegan and cruelty free. They are made in the USA. I included the ingredients at the end of my review.

You get 3 glosses - a peachy pink toned one that has a strawberry scent/flavor, a more reddish toned one that has a grape scent/flavor, and a clear one that has a bubble gum scent/flavor. As for the scents, I can notice them when applying, not so much after the fact. I don’t really notice a taste really with any of them, but the ingredients do list “flavor” not fragrance/parfum, so it made me think they’d have more taste to them. Not a big deal to me though.

While 2 of them are tinted in color, the color doesn’t really translate onto the lips. They just look clear on the lips. They don’t change any color of lip liner, lipstick, matte lip, etc. that I put them over top of. So, I liked that.

The formula is nice. It feels nice and moisturizing on the lips, which I expected given they have nice moisturizing ingredients like Shea butter, coconut oil, and squalane. They do feel SLIGHTLY sticky on the lips. I hate sticky glosses, but these aren’t overly sticky. They don’t feel thick or heavy on the lips. They feel nice and thin. They are glosses so they do need re-applied often throughout the day. But, I’m used to that with glosses so it’s not a big deal to me.

The strawberry and grape both have micro glitter in them (mica). I didn’t know that when I got these. None of the product photos showed they had glitter and the description didn’t say it either. I’m not a fan of micro glitter in my lip glosses. I feel once the gloss wears off, it usually leaves behind glitter particles on my lips. Also, they can sometimes feel gritty. So, I was surprised when I saw 2 of the 3 had glitter. Luckily, these didn’t feel gritty. I do notice that there are some glitter particles on my lips after they wear off, but it’s not too bad. The glitter particles are really small, so it’s not really noticeable. So, I don’t mind that.

Overall, these are nice glosses with a nice formula. They might seem pricey when seeing the $30 price tag, but you’re getting 3 tubes of glosses, so that’s only $10 a piece, which isn’t bad. Of course, it isn’t great. You can get a tube of gloss that looks great and works just as well from the drugstore for slightly less. But, the ingredients might make it worth it to spend the little more. I give them 4 stars.

INGREDIENTS
Bubblegum Lip Glossy:
Hydrogenated Polyisobutene, Cocos Nucifera (Coconut) Oil*, Isododecane, Ethylene/Propylene/Styrene Copolymer, Flavor, Sorbitan Oleate, Squalane, Butylene/Ethylene/Styrene Copolymer, Tocopherol, Butyrospermum Parkii (Shea) Butter Extract, Tocopheryl Acetate (Vitamin E Acetate).

*Organic/Biologique.

Grape Lip Glossy:
Hydrogenated Polyisobutene, Cocos Nucifera (Coconut) Oil*, Isododecane, Ethylene/Propylene/Styrene Copolymer, Flavor, Sorbitan Oleate, Squalane, Butylene/Ethylene/Styrene Copolymer, Tocopherol, Ricinus Communis (Castor) Seed Oil, Butyrospermum Parkii (Shea) Butter Extract, Mica, Titanium Dioxide, Tocopheryl Acetate (Vitamin E Acetate), Iron Oxides (CI 77492), Red 33 (CI#17200), CI 15850 (Red 7), Iron Oxides (CI 77499).

*Organic/Biologique.

Strawberry Lip Glossy:
Hydrogenated Polyisobutene, Cocos Nucifera (Coconut) Oil*, Isododecane, Ethylene/Propylene/Styrene Copolymer, Flavor, Sorbitan Oleate, Squalane, Butylene/Ethylene/Styrene Copolymer, Tocopherol, Titanium Dioxide, Butyrospermum Parkii (Shea) Butter Extract, Calcium Aluminum Borosilicate, Ricinus Communis (Castor) Seed Oil, Mica, Tocopheryl Acetate (Vitamin E Acetate), Iron Oxides (CI 77492), Red 27 Lake (CI 45410), Silica, Tin Oxide.

*Organic/Biologique

This is a really great quality set of brushes. I am a mineral makeup user, and struggle to find brushes that pick up the right amount of product without being wasteful. I have several brands of brushes, some purchased from amazon and some purchased piece by piece in Ulta or Sephora and these brushes are the same type of quality you would expect to find in a beauty store. You get 12 brushes and a very nicely made carrying case, I actually split the brushes into face and eye and keep the case open as holders placing eye brushes in one and face brushes in the other. I like that the seller addresses the smell issue head on, they include it in the instructions, you need to air out the brushes for 2 days and then gently wash the bristles, I find that philosophy brand face wash is what I prefer to use as a weekly washing for the bristles it really restores and removed the makeup that accumulated thru out the week. I will breakdown the brushes and how I feel they perform.
1. Large Powder – perfect for my setting powder to complete the look.
2. Large Angled Contour - great for both blush and bronzer
3. Duo Fiber – use for stippling on my powder foundation
4. Foundation – applying foundation to my hairline and around my nose
5. Large Shader – works very well, picks up product without being wasteful
6. Concealer – smallish but so are the spots it needs to hit!
7. Medium Angled Shading – use for my eyebrow powder
8. Angled Shadow – great for my crease
9. Tapered Blending – awesome at working out the edges of the lid
10. Small Angle – use for my top lid eyeliner
11. Eye Liner – use for my bottom liner, fits well under the lash line
12. Pencil Brush – use it wet when I want to darken my mineral liner

The delicately-scented lavender and vanilla shave cream is my favorite out of at least 10 different soaps and creams I have used. It lathers quickly, goes on smoothly using my silvertip badger brush, and forms a slick, creamy substance. Shaving with Apothecary 87 is a pleasure.

Lavender is my #1 go-to essential oil. I think I use it in 90% of my blends, whether in the diffuser or in topical blends (or sprays, etc). The only thing I definitely don’t use it for is my home-made cleaning products where citrus oils are the only essential oils.

This particular oil is a very delicate scent – some lavender oils can pack quite a punch in terms of the scent. This is certainly floral and has a ‘high note’ to it. You can easily pick it out of a blend. It’s my first oil from New York Biology and I’m very pleased.

I love blending lavender in particular with chamomile, as it has an almost instantaneous calming effect. Also love to blend it with bergamot, ylang ylang, mandarin, and a drop of lime.

The bottle is very large, a dark brown color to protect the oil from UV rays which can damage the oils and decrease the shelf life. My favorite way to dispense oils is with the eye dropper (the euro droppers found in smaller bottles tend to gush several drops at once, so that’s definitely not my preference) – you can really control how many drops you dispense with these eye droppers.

Arrived well protected in bubble wrap and there was no leaks.

* I received the oil for evaluation and review

My daughter and I discovered facial sheet masks over the summer and have become fans of them. They are easy and fun to use. This box of Anti-Aging sheet masks comes with 5 masks. Each mask is individually wrapped. When you open up a mask and remove it from the packing, you see that there are 2 layers. One is the sheet mask which is thin and papery like. The other is a plastic type material. The two layers are together, so I put both of them on my face. The other masks that I have used didn't have this plastic layer. I couldn't get it to lay flat on my skin very well, so I peeled the plastic layer off leaving just the paper one. This was what I was more familiar with. That layer was easy to mold to my face. The mask was very cold which felt refreshing. It's very wet - not to the point of dripping off my face, but enough that you want to spread it around more on the mask as it is on your face. It says to leave it on for 15-20 minutes. I opted for the lesser time because my coffee was ready & I didn't want to wait any longer for it! Then you are supposed to rub any left over "goo" (for a lack of a better word) into you skin. I accidently got a little into my eye, but it didn't burn or sting at all, which surprised me. I have very sensitive eyes and was expecting to feel some pain. I regularly use anti-aging products, plus I have only used one of these sheet masks so far, so I don't notice any difference to my skin, but I would definitely add this to my beauty routine.

*I purchased these masks at a reduced price for the purpose of trying out & reviewing. My thoughts & opinions are my own.

So it's honestly not bad at this price point. It sheds a bit but all units have some shedding. For the mix brown I give 4 stars.The face front is about 2 inches and is meant to be cut and plucked for a natural look. I did neither in the photo. I just folded it under the the front as I'm afraid that cutting will make it look lifted in the front and obviously show it's a wig without some glue to put it down. The color is not exactly what I wanted. I was hoping it would be a darker brown. It's kinda a mousey brown and alittle strange in my opinion. It will need to be restyle in the front as some waves/curls flattened out by how it was packed in transport. The part is both a plastic fake skin and a mesh on top. You can see the mesh upon close examination. Makeup might help to conceal this.... Or make it worse. What I did like was the fullness and length. The weft was not too easy to be seen even when pulling hair apart. This is good compared to other brands where the wefts are often very visible, especially in the back and crown of a unit. Because the simulated scalp is narrow, you won't be able to change the part. It's also a bit flat near the part so it's gonna take some work to give a bit of volume. I would have enjoyed this much more had the color been a rich brown and if the scalp was a bit more realistic. Would I wear in public? Probably not. Would this be good for photo shoots or costumes ? yes. Would I buy other colors from this brand? Yes and I haven't done that with other brands on Amazon. For the purple ombre: I give 3 stars. It doesn't have the lace in front. This is more pure costume. The black is very shiney and the front of wig where the part is shows a bit of the black backing for construction. If it laid more flat and had the lace I would have given the purple ombre 4 stars. Dull the shiney with some powder like dry shampoo or maybe even dark eyeshadow. The fake scalp is actually better on the purple ombre compared to the brown mix. The ombre for the blonde is also a 4 star. Hair at the crown is a bit flat but the rest of the style is beautiful. The brown to blonde has the 2 inch lace like the long brown one. It's not as full on the head but you don't see the wefts unless you pull it apart a bit.  I feel the color is much more realistic. Hair feels pretty good for a synthetic. Note if you play too much , it will get a knotty feel. Again, good for photos. Some people might actually wear it daily but I don't know if it took take daily abuse. Length is great.

***update*** 3/29/17

So after my initial review on this cup expressing my extreme disappointment with the so-called spill proof lid, I was contacted buy a customer representative from Gus. He expressed his apologies and they ended up refunding my total purchase price even after I told them they didn't have to and sent me a replacement lid.

Unfortunately they did not understand my complaint was not about the rubber gasket around the lid itself but the slider that covers the drinking hole. It is the slider that leaks because it does not seal. I informed them of my opinion on the design, which is I believe is a poor design for what is supposed to be a spill free top.

Having said that, the Cup itself is absolutely fantastic. It's well-made and does indeed keep my coffee hot for quite a while. I have not tested it with cold yet. The way Gus stands behind their product definitely tells me that there concern is for customer satisfaction.

They were very prompt and courteous. I really can't ask any more of them. I have put the rating up to 4. If nothing else it's because of the simple fact they reached out to me to try and make it right. That to me speaks volumes of this company's commitment to ensuring their customers are happy. That's not something you see a whole lot of these days.

If they can come up with a design that makes the lid truly spill free I would definitely buy this again.

I’ve been using natural fiber loofahs (not the plastic kind) off and on since 2010. By nature there is some variation, depending on the size of the underlying plant, the way it’s made/grown, any bleaching, softening or other treatments applied that may change the texture. This is GREAT value for three medium-to-small sized natural loofahs.

The first time you use it you gotta soak it in warm water to puff up (it will turn into an oval cylinder, rather than staying like a flat pad) and release its seeds. I didn’t find the seed quantity to be gross or unreasonable, though it’s definitely different than most natural loofahs you’ll buy in a drugstore or Whole Foods, say—those tend to be treated and at least prewashed, to remove the seeds etc.

The smell: I have a super sensitive nose. Just speaking for the item I received, this is 100% “normal loofah smell” —a little grassy, kind of like dried clean straw that’s been dampened again—with a bit of a smoky smell. It’s not offensively smoky. Like a lot less than most whiskey. No “funk,” though the natural loofah herbaceous-ness does get stronger when it’s wet.

I didn’t find this too scratchy at all—in fact, by the time I’d finished soaking out the seeds I found it a little soft for my liking. To be fair I do drybrush about once a week, so I’m used to a good scrub. But if you know what a “normal store-bought loofah” feels like, this is no scratchier than that. If you have super sensitive skin, try using this after it’s soaked in a bowl or tub of hot(!) water for 10 min or so, with some emollient body wash like Aveenk.

Definitely did not see what the hype was about. This diffuser, at a minimum, creates a slight blockage to the air flow of the blow dryer. It did attach to the my blow dryer easily, but it felt cheap and continued to collapse while in use. As a curly girl, I like quality air distribution to get the job done. This didn't do for me- returned.

**Edit - Update - it's been a week, and the red that had previously lightened to strawberry blonde is now returning in full force. I'm so disappointed. While it wasn't too horrible on my hair, I hesitate to try it a third time and risk major damage for a product that clearly isn't all I thought it would be. I am sad to reduce this from 5 stars down to 2, since it did reduce the color slightly.

It's important to note that I didn't bleach my hair at any time, so my base is all grey. My intention was to remove the red, and eventually lighten my hair down to the grey and stop coloring for good. I used the product twice, one day leaving it in for the 20 minutes, then washing with a clarifying shampoo, followed by a 20 volume developer diluted 1:1 with water as this product recommended on the box. Honestly, my hair darkened after the developer, so when I used the product again the next morning, I repeated all the steps minus the developer, then followed the instructions for a purple shampoo. I also have a good quality hair mask, and Olaplex No. 6 & 7 products to help minimize the damage. My biggest fear was being left with a hideous orange color, or a bright sun yellow, which didn't happen. I'm pretty happy with the results, since I have heard how hard it is to get red out. The smell isn't nearly as bad as some of the reviews made it out to be, and the product was very easy to use. I did wrap my hair in plastic wrap, and tried to keep it warm with a towel to improve the results. I may give it another go just to get closer to the grey, but I'm going to give it some time so I don't damage my hair. Definitely glad I went this route instead of something that may have damaged my hair.

Upgraded to this razor after my old Remington died, and I've been very happy with it. It cut a bit close, which irritated my skin at first, but I think that was just me getting used to the "Lift & cut."

This would be an excellent razor if it wasn't for two features (or lack thereof) that make it a pain to use.

1. The internal battery is NiCd. This type of battery has "memory" to it, which means for optimal performance, the battery should be fully drained before you recharge it. Otherwise, the life of the battery will slowly shorten until the battery is useless.

2. There isn't a low battery indicator.

By itself, either of these features wouldn't pose a problem. Combined, you're left wondering when you'll have to charge the shaver, or when you're off on that business trip, if you'll need to bring the charger.

In the long run, this razor is cheap enough and I expect the battery to last for years. Still, it would have been better if the battery was made of NiMH or some technology that's not 15 years old.

What an incredible display of motion on screen. That Iron Monkey has amazing fight choreography is a given but what amazes me many times in these movies is how even with all the non-stop action they still seem to get in some descent character development. Let's face it. You can have the best fights ever but if you don't care at all about the people it's only good for one viewing and we're here looking for multiple viewing, right kids!
The chinese actors are incredibly adept at using few words and clever use of body language to convey as much in one scene that Stallone or Willis can't produce in an entire movie. The problem in the U.S. is that acting is not really infused with any real physical ability like in Asia. Often from a young age martial arts are a part of a performer or actors life. Some more than others. Someone in the US who has studied for the physical abilities probably can't act their way out of a paper bag because it hasn't been a part of growing up for them. The prime example being good ol' Chuck Norris.
Just say thanks that we in the U.S. can finally get good access to these action masterpieces. This movie is Fist of Legend's equal in fight scenes but surpasses it in natural humour.
If you want to experience another great HK movie that's in a modern setting and not a period piece try Tsui Hark's Time and Tide. Not as action orientated but with great characters and slightly more realistic sequences.

I have not tried expensive sunscreens, so I can't compare there, but for the price per ounce, this is definitely the best sunscreen I've found--a holy grail. It does not feel like a sunscreen. It doesn't leave me greasy or stay wet all day, even when I use the proper amount--1/2 tsp for full face and neck. It does NOT leave a white cast at all despite coming out of the bottle white. Unlike the satiny Trader Joe's daily sunscreen, which is a bit pricier, I don't even feel the need to wipe it off my palms/fingers after applying. It dries without drying out--like moisturizer; I barely feel I have anything on my face--again, just like moisturizer. I can apply it over a moisturizer plus azelaic acid, and I DON'T get pilling. After several hours, I notice a bit of a shine, which is natural for my oily skin--you may want the Trader Joe's one if you're oily and want to be matte, which is more drying and actually a gel that doesn't run. This Skin Aqua one is pretty liquidy, but not messy due to the narrow applicator. The scent is a bit odd; others here have said like alcohol, which I guess might be accurate. I think I stopped noticing it after 3 applications, and the scent does NOT linger after applying. It is fragrance-free--good for sensitive skin and prevention of developing allergies. It's also small enough I can keep it in my jacket or hoodie pocket to reapply throughout the day without being so small that I go through it in a week (3.88 oz).

When applying HA with my fingers it always took a minute or two to soak in, not so with this sprayer the results were immediate! Although the Nano mist is so fine its impossible to see, the end results is an amazing feeling of soft fully moisturized skin.

With this sprayer you can chose to use serums, toners or water so your not limited to only water like other Nano sprayers. This sprayer is well made and the USB cord is included, the liquid chamber is small [perhaps two tablespoon capacity] easy to fill and it only used a tiny amount of serum to cover my face and neck.

The only Cons are the USB port's location on the bottom so it must be laid on its side to charge {that's weird} and the instruction booklet could use a little help. But, as stated at the beginning of this review I'm so impressed with how my skin feels I'm forgiving the aforementioned transgressions and giving this a 5 star rating.

Honestly, I don't normally get excited about skin care tools but this one is an exception and its made me a Nano spray believer. Highly recommend.

I have pretty small, hooded eyes, so it’s hard to find a lash curler that works for my eyes. The biggest issue I have is them pinching my eye when I’m clamping down to curl them. I always have to lightly clamp down to see if I’ll pinch myself and, if I will, then adjust. Then, I end up having some parts not curled. I’d been looking for a new curler for a while, when I saw this I figured it was worth a try since I also got mascara.

Someone in the reviews had said the package came damaged. Mine also did. It wasn’t too bad, just seemed a little beat up. But, the products inside were completely fine and didn’t seem damaged at all, so I was okay with that.

I am very picky about my mascara. Well, not so much the mascara as I am the mascara wand. With my small, hooded eyes, it makes it so I end up with a lot of mistakes of the wand hitting below my lower lash line or onto my hood. So, I prefer thinner wands. I wasn’t sure what to expect with this mascara, but I was also getting a lash curler so I figured I’d hopefully like at least 1 of them. I’m happy to say that the mascara wand isn’t too thick. It’s not extremely thin, but still thin enough so it works for me. It also has a little bend to it, which helps with application. The mascara goes on very easily and doesn’t clump up after a few coats, which is great! It stays on all day without smearing. But, it also washes off easily at the end of the day, which I always love. It’s hard to find a mascara that stays all day without smearing or smudging, but is easy to wash off. So, it’s nice this does both!

The lash curler is amazing! It’s lightweight. It feels so nice. I love the red, black, and gold. It looks so fancy! It fits my eyes really well and curls my lashes AMAZINGLY without pinching my eyes at all! I’m really happy with it! It also comes with replacements for the rubber part on the curler, so that was great!

For $11.99 (at the time of writing this), it’s an amazing deal! You’re getting an amazing lash curler and an amazing mascara. You can’t beat that! If you’re looking for a great mascara, a great lash curler, or both, you should definitely give this a try!

This is really a very unique travel/gift set. The items included truly make it handy when traveling and the items are not typically found bundled together. There are a few items that I really wanted. A) It has a glass nail file with a carrying case. This glass file is very nice and they last for such a long time that this item alone was worth the set. What I really like about a crystal/glass nail file is that you can clean and sanitize it. Besides maintaining its use for much longer periods than regular nail files, the fact that I can clean this is a huge bonus for me. B) The travel perfume atomizer. This is just fantastic for travel/storing in a small bag, etc... it is super simple to fill (fill from the bottom if you've never used one before and can be confusing at first) and there is no need to carry around a full bottle of my favorite perfume. There is a small window so that you can easily identify when it is time to refill. The tweezers set with a built in light is actually very nice. It is comfortable to hold/use and the light is useful. Now these may not end up being your "go-to" tweezers for eyebrow maintenance but they are a fairly good set. The make up brush is a good quality as well and stores away without leaving/getting make up in your purse. As someone that is not typically a fan of 'gift sets', I will say that this is a very nice set.The fact that the items are not typically seen bundled together, makes it one I would enjoy giving as a gift

This is a very thin, clear oil. It isn’t as thick as baby oil and runs almost like water. I would never put oil or an oil-based product on my skin because it clogs the pores. Some people might be able to get away with it, but even though I have dry skin, my skin will break out if I put oil on it, so I have skipped that test. I did rub it into my hands and it dries fast without leaving an oily slick.

What I did use this for is my hair, which is very thick and very dry. After shampooing, I poured a little bit of the oil into my hand and rubbed my hands together. Then I ran my hands through my hair, keeping the oil away from the scalp area. Afterwards, I blew my hair dry then applied more oil. Usually, I apply a gel in the same manner that calms the frizz and leaves my hair with a nice shine to it. However, the oil did not produce the same results. My hair still had flyaway ends with no gloss to it. My newly washed and dried hair always looks better the next morning after the hair product has had a chance to calm the frizzy hair shafts, but the morning after I used the oil, the results were no different than the night before.

I’ll stick to my gel product to end the frizziness of dry hair.

I bought this toothbrush because of all the statistics singing the praises of electric toothbrushes. I thought that this head looked particularly effective, so I placed my order.
This is a great toothbrush. Some people have complained about the head size, but that's practically an Oral B trademark, and after a week or so you don't even notice. The toothbrush itself is pretty intense. It might hurt your gums the first time you use it. It is fast and powerful and it really gets the job done. The battery will last almost a week without charging, but after a few days you start to steadily lose power. I recommend keeping it charged most of the time. It's not particularly loud for a brush of its kind, but it does make some noise.
My only complaint: the vibrations sometimes bother my lips and/or nose. It's not as noticeable after awhile, but it's a bit annoying at first. Still, that's not really the toothbrush's fault. It can't help being that intense.
A final comment: Don't listen to some of the negative comments. A lot of them happened because the person didn't read the instructions before use. If used properly, the brush is a huge improvement over a manual. I think my dentist will appreciate it. Five stars.

I found this hair to be quite good for the price. The texture is smooth and straight, silky but not fragile. The texture and shine is pretty realistic--maybe a bit on the shiny side--but since the hair is of a uniform colour, you'd have buy multiple colours and mix them in order to avoid the flat "bad dye job" look. The feel is a bit plasticy, but wigs aren't for touching anyway. The hair is resilient, and doesn't tangle easily or pick up static. The colour (dark auburn) was a fair bit darker than shown on the photo. I have a professionally-calibrated monitor for graphic design, so if the photo colours are off, it's not on my end. That said, I can understand if they did that on purpose: The hair itself is so dark that it would show up black on a lot of people's monitors if it they didn't lighten up the photo a bit. The quantity of hair is pretty good for the price, but I find I'd rather pay a little more for the bundle to include just a bit more hair--the amount per bundle is juuuuust a tad too thin to make a satisfying extension or ponytail, so you'd end up having to buy two and having a bunch leftover. Lame.

I received my order within four days. The hair had been folded up a little too tight in the packaging, so when I first unboxed it, it had a couple of kinks that fell out on their own during the wigmaking process.

The reason I'm giving it a four is because of the lies and deceit about this hair being suitable for curling. Granted, you should never take a styling iron to synthetic hair no matter how heat-resistant it claims to be, but I had that previously-mentioned "bunch leftover" so I gave it a shot on a low temperature. The hair came out melted and frizzy-but still straight. Why god why would you tell people that their hair can be heat-styled if heat styling will ruin their hair? For the wig itself, I used the standard steam-styling method, which worked just fine and didn't damage the hair.

Overall, I think this is a great powder! I love how finely milled it is! It’s so soft and velvety on the skin! It doesn’t look like I have powder on, but sets my makeup in place. It looks and feels nice on the skin. You get a good amount of product. I’m really happy with it! The only thing I don’t like is how hard the lid is to twist initially. I also don’t like the product page talking about natural ingredients - baichi flower seed oil and jojoba seed oil - being in the powder and that not being on the ingredient list. So, I took a star off for that. But, other than that, I recommend this powder. If like me, you don’t usually like powders and the only ones you do like are finely milled, you’ll love this powder! If you’ve had trouble finding powders that work for you. If they tend to make your skin look dry, powdery, or cakey. I recommend trying this one out!

This comes in an outer product box. It is made in China. It has a cruelty free logo (PETA). The ingredients are listed on the back. It includes mica and parabens.

It’s odd because the product page states that it’s made of “natural safe ingredients” and has “baichi flower seed oil water-oil balance, long-lasting makeup” and “jojoba seed oil moisturize the skin,” but neither of those ingredients are found in the ingredient list on the back of the product box. So, I don’t understand where that comes from? I hate when there’s what seems like blatantly false claims on a product page. It makes me not trust anything from the brand.

The jar of powder is square. It’s a good size. It is plastic. The bottom is clear but frosted and matte. The top has the same design as the front of the box. It has a nice look to it.

I hate to say it, but how to get the top off had me a little confused at first 🙈 I initially thought it would twist off, but when I went to twist it, it didn’t seem to want to move. Then, I thought, well since it’s square, maybe it will pull off. So, I carefully tried to pull the top off, but it didn’t want to budge. Then, I tried twisting it again, this time really putting effort into it and it finally moved and I got it off. At first it seemed like it only went halfway, but wouldn’t twist any more (again, I was being careful because I didn’t want powder getting everywhere). Then, I just lifted it up and it came off. I thought after the first time it would be easier to get the top off, but it wasn’t. You still really need to put some elbow grease into it every single time, which is annoying. If you have trouble with things like that, this might not be the powder for you. There was a clear sticker covering the holes, which I always appreciate! So, had I yanked the top off, Powder wouldn’t have gotten everywhere! But, you never know since some brands don’t do that! But, I’m glad this one did!

Another thing, I kind of wish the part with the holes, where the powder is, I wish it was square like the jar. I always dump powder into the lid and dip my brush into that. Then, whatever powder I have left, I dump back into the jar. Usually, I can just take the cap with the leftover powder and carefully put it on and the powder will fall into the main part. With this, if I do that the powder gets down on the edges of the jar (see photos), so I always have to make sure to carefully dump the powder back in before putting the lid on.

I had a hard time deciding which shade I wanted to get. Usually, I go with translucent. But, sometimes I like a little extra coverage from a skin toned powder. But, this powder had an option for a pink powder, and I always heard pink was good for brightening, so then I thought about that one! The descriptions on the middle of the product page didn’t really help. For #1, “Ivory White,” it said, “Suitable for fair skin. Create white makeup.” I was initially thinking this one was translucent, until I read the description. I’m fair skinned, but I don’t know what they mean by white makeup. But, it seemed like it was going to have a white tint to it, which I didn’t want. For #2, “Natural Color” they had, “Suitable for any race. Create light makeup.” If it’s “suitable for any race” that would imply it’s translucent, but it didn’t look translucent. It looked like a fair skin toned color. For #3, “Soft Pink,” it had, “Suitable for pink skin. Create a glossy, ruddy makeup.” I have fair skin and I’m cool toned, so pink skin makes sense. Glossy doesn’t seem too bad, but I’m not sure what they meant by that. Do they mean it’s going to give a glow? And who wants ruddy makeup? See how confused I was! 🤦‍♀️

I thought I ended up going with #2 “Natural Color,” and the box mine came in had “#2” on the top flap, but when I looked at the color of the powder through the frosted clear bottom it looked soft pink. When I first dumped some out into the lid it looked soft pink. But, when I swatched it, it seemed more of a fair skin toned color. So, I THINK I got the right shade (#2 Natural Color). It definitely works for my fair skin. But, I don’t see how this would be “suitable for any race.” To me, it seems like it blends out MOSTLY transparent. But, it does seem like there might be a HINT of fair shade/white to it, so I’m not sure if it will work for medium to darker skin tones.

The powder is very finely milled. It has almost a velvety feel when you touch it. I am 35, have normal to dry skin with some extremely dry areas, and suffer from hormonal acne. In my experience, most powders make my skin look dry or powdery or cakey, so I only like using powder in specific places where I absolutely need it. Even then, there’s only a handful of powders I like for that purpose that look good on my skin. This has become one of those! Out of the handful of powders I like on my skin, the one thing they all have in common is that they are all VERY finely milled. I find that the more fine the powder is, the less it looks like you have powder on your face. It just sets my makeup in place in my oily areas without making it look dry/powdery/cakey.

These spray bottles are just what we needed. We use spray bottles for a variety of purposes, and after putting these to the test we were happy with the results.

First was for spray cleaner. We sometimes mix our own natural cleaners, and they worked great for that purpose. No muss, no fuss, just good even sprays. They are ideal for this.

Next, I use a soapy mixture to control pests in my garden. Same results here. The bottle sprayed nicely and evenly, with no leaks or clogs. (I'm sure if there was just water in the bottle, it'd be perfect.)

The last use was the trickiest: a water and white glue mixture used in crafting. Things were a little more fussy here, but still usable. The spray worked, but the nozzle would get minor clog after a few sprays, sending the spray in uneven, odd directions. A bit of warm water would clear it up, then it would clog again. Still, this was water and GLUE, so it's not uncommon for some bottles to be fussy with such a mix.

The squeeze action works pretty well, simple and smooth. The plastic is a slightly harder plastic, more prone to breaks if squeezed than softer bottles. The manufacturing quality is on the cheaper side. They work well, but are not made for wear and tear.

Overall, getting six of these for a ten spot is a pretty good deal, so they get a thumbs up from me, with the caveat that they won't take a lot of punishment.

I have been curious about these heartless curling rod headbands for a while now. I mean who wouldn’t love to wake up with curled and bouncy hair that you didn’t even have to use heat on?!?! So when I came across this set I figured it was totally worth a try!

I’m the set you get the shiny metallic/holographic bag, 2 scrunchies, one hair clip, and the long curling thing itself. Both the scrunchies and the curling rod thing have a satin finish, which is great for your hair and is soft to the touch. The curling rod thing seams to be stuffed with basic stuffing like you would find in a cheap pillow. Honestly the overall quality is just ok, nothing fancy.

I have used this multiple times now, and there is for sure a learning curve. It seams simple enough, but the way you wrap it, how tightly you wrap it, how big the sections are that you wrap, and how damp your hair is all play a huge rule in how it turns out. They do include a little card with pictures on the basics of how to wrap your hair, but the rest you will have to figure out as you go.

As far as how well it works, it definitely does work. Even my hair that usually doesn’t hold a curl with heat I was able to get decent wavy styles out of. This does give a nice wave to it, however if I want truly curly hair or even loose curls then this doesn’t do that for me.

One other thing worth the mentioning, I priced these out and this set is 2x-3x more expensive than others sold on here. And being that the quality on this isn’t amazing or anything. Im confused as to why. But overall it’s an ok product.

I'm very interested in trying new beauty products to pamper myself, and I have some dry skin on my face that I'm always struggling to keep in check, so it sounded appealing to give this waterproof facial brush cleaning system a try to compare and contrast to other similar products I own.

This skin cleansing kit comes with one waterproof and cordless rotating machine, one facial brush, one large body brush, one serum and moisturizer applicator sponge, and one pumice stone. This machine requires four AA batteries to run, which aren't included.

The brushes feel pleasant to the touch, and I liked that four different attachment heads were included. They are soft, but have just enough pressure to them to feel like they will exfoliate dirt, oil, and dead skin well. The set-up for this cleaning system is simple and straightforward, but I found it quite difficult to open the battery compartment on the end of the handle.

Once the batteries were inserted successfully and the battery compartment was snapped back into place, all that I needed to do was to pick a brush head and push it in to the attachment point. Then you can turn the unit on or off by pressing one of two buttons on the blue section of the brush. In my experience with the brushes, they didn't rotate evenly, and the brushes wobbled as they rotated. I'm not sure if this is an intended feature or not, but I'm suspecting it simply means the platforms for the brushes aren't quite even.

I'm glad that this cleaning system is waterproof, but I'll admit I was a bit underwhelmed that it only has one speed, and that that speed is rather slow. I still feel like I will get some good use out of it, but I would have liked if there were more speed options, because for attachments like the pumice stone, you really need a slightly faster speed for it to perform really well.

That said, I was generally pleased with the accessories offered in the kit, but would have liked if they included a small storage or travel bag of some kind.

Overall, I like this waterproof electric facial brush, and would recommend it if you're considering adding one to your own health and beauty routine. Just keep in mind it has only one slow speed, and that you will need to provide your own storage bag and batteries.

I am a longtime Amazon Prime customer who writes reviews in my free time. I was appreciative to be given an opportunity to test and review this waterproof facial brush cleaning brush complimentary for my honest and unbiased review. If you have any questions or comments about this product or my review, feel free to message me and I'll be glad to help!

I gotta start off my review by telling you to be very cautious when purchasing these face mask. Because the photo shows you 4 different types of mask, but based on your selection you will only be getting one box of face mask. Inside this box it's the same type of mask and no other variety.

I gotta say I do love using sheet mask, they always provide my skin the extra glow and moisture it needs during the harsh winters. These face mask have plenty of serum on the mask and inside the packaging that you can lather on your skin. I purchased the aqua pack because I needed more moisture overall. Each of the different mask has a different purpose so read the description before you make your selection.

Each of the mask are individually wrapped so they're perfect to take on the go or in an overnight bag. All you need to do is open and unwrap the folded mask and align it with your face. Compared to many other sheet mask, these fit my face shape perfectly without having all these air bubbles. Leave it on your face for 15-20 minutes and then rub the additional juices and slap your face slightly so it absorbed completely. You don't need to wash your face again after using the mask.

I wanted to use this cream for a couple of weeks after receiving it, then write my review....I've used quite a few "natural" products! That's always been my thing, natural, or organic even better!...this one is fantastic! One warming, if you're looking for something completely weightless, this might not be for you. (Depending on the amount you use.) However, now that I'm past a certain age, I like a little something more! This cream is very smooth and absorbs great! I ordered it because I wanted a day cream with a bit of SPF for summer, but I like it as a day and night cream both! My makeup also goes on nicely with this underneath. Makes my skin feel like velvet! Other all natural creams of this same type that I've used in the past tend to sometimes have a grittiness. (Only a guess, but I'm thinking shea butter must sometimes separate and get tiny chunks.) NOT the case here at all! This cream is very homogenized. There is just a very slight hint of an herb-y scent, almost undetectable, which I also like!  The only down side to this product might be the price. Honestly, I don't usually pay this much for a small jar of skin cream, but, as mentioned, a little goes a long way. I use a dab somewhat larger than a pea for each use, so the jar should last awhile!...Shipping and packaging was also excellent!

Of the choices available I chose to take the Aqua mask. I found it to be very nicely hydrating, and was full of plenty of serum on the mask itself. Application of the mask is pretty straightforward, you just align it with your face according to the pattern on the mask, and allow it to sit for 15 to 20 minutes. I always like to apply the mask right after my shower before bed. After I've washed my face in the shower, I will apply the mask before any of the rest of my nighttime skin care routine. When I would use the mask, I would skip the serum part of my nighttime routine. I found that applying serum after the mask is simply redundant an unnecessary. After removing the mask there was always plenty of extra serum that would sit atop my skin, so I would rub the rest of it in gently with my hands until it's saturated which didn't take very long as the serum I found saturated very nicely. The mask did provide a very nice amount of moisture to my otherwise dry skin. I tend to get a dry spot or two on my face from time to time, particularly around the chin area, and I found that after wearing the mask I would not suffer from that issue. It left my skin smooth and with a nice afterglow as well. All in all I was very satisfied with my experience, so I would recommend giving them a try. A nice added bonus is that they are very affordable.

I know this is a baby bath oil, but I'm a fan of using bath oils as shave oils. A little goes a long way, just a few drops per leg (or you can add a drop to your regular shave gel). Helps reduce irritation and you can see where you are shaving! I can't live without it. It's better than any foam, gel or lotion on the market. Truly the best shave experience. I have thick hair and dry flaky skin that is prone to itching and sensitivity. I use this bath oil with a cheapie disposable razor and a few drops of body oil after a shower and my legs are silky for days. Literally days. No razor burn, no irritation, no frustration. I prefer unscented products and this is truly unscented--thank you! Please use caution when using oils in the tub, but I haven't had any problems with the tub getting slippery. Only thing I would change in a better bottle with a pump or dropper top. It's easy to use too much. A little goes a long way so if you're hesitant about the price, don't be, because you'll end up spending more in the long run on something cheaper.

Also, used this as a body wash after getting a little too much sun today, and my skin was super soothed. I think it will help prevent my skin from developing a sunburn--bonus! I may just have to buy a vat of this!

I was not impressed with this foot mask. It claims to be exfoliating, so I expected it to work like other foot exfoliating masks I’ve used in the past. The other foot masks are used like these are - there’s a gel distributed inside some plastic booties. You open the top of the booties, put your feet in the booties, massage the gel through the bootie so your entire foot is covered with gel, tape the bootie closed around your ankle them sit back and relax for an hour or so. The other masks Ive used took about three days for the exfoliation to start - I would shed thin sheets of skin for several days and at the end be left with amazingly soft uncalloused feet. This did not happen with this product. No shedding, no exfoliation. My feet may have been a little softer immediately after washing off the product, but that is the only benefit I received. I thought maybe I didn’t do it right with the first set in this 3-pack, so a month later I got out another pair of booties, read the instructions super carefully, left on the booties for the longest recommenced time and waited to see if I’d get better results this time. Nope. Will go back to ordering one of my previous brands

Against my better judgement, my impatience overcame me and I ordered these hair extensions because of the competitive price and prime eligibility. They are absolutely SYNTHETIC hair, hence the ad not stating that it is 100% human remy hair. I had ordered the sunny brand hair a few weeks prior and there's no comparison in quality. In the attached photos you will see the real hair on the left (with the tape) and the obvious synthetic hair on the right after flat ironing both at the same heat level. The moment I saw these extensions even still in the package I instantly recognized the fake shine typical in synthetic hair and still opened one package and ran my flat iron through the hair just to see how it would react. The flat iron was almost sticking to the hair instead of gliding though and left behind stripes of faded color and left them feeling dry and rough. I am a professional stylist and I know the difference between real and fake hair. They also come with NO tape on them so if you do buy youll have to buy your own (spend that money on better extensions) If I could give this product no stars I absolutely would. I feel bad for those who can't tell the difference and waste their time and money on extensions that will last maybe a month and paying $40 per pack for something they could easily find for less than  $5 a pack. Returning tomorrow. Do not buy this product. I'm updating my review to 3 stars because they provided excellent customer service and refunded me right away this was most likely a bad batch of hair from their manufacturer.

This wax is my go to wax for my mustache. I have a thinner mustache with thin tips and this holds beautiful curls all day long as long as you don't get it wet. It will hold up to my dog's occasional kiss, but anything more, like rain, and they will fall apart immediately. With this said it makes it really easy to clean up at the end of the day. It does tend to leave droplets on the sink, but they wipe off with a damp rag.

This wax does have a dye in it, and it does cover the few greys i have in my mustache. The brown was too dark, this seems to blend a bit better and look more natural with my brown facial hair.

I have attached 2 photos so you can see the length of the mustache and the normal size of my everyday curls. If I am dressing up I will take the time to make larger curls, but they are more delicate and don't hold as long.

I find this to be super easy to keep with me, as you may find throughout the day that you may have to touch up your mustache for various reasons. The tube makes it extremely simple to do so.

With that said you do need to have water with you when you do the mustache as if you use your finger, which you will have to do to get curls, you will have to apply it with your fingers and they will be covered in the dye.

The dye is not permanent and has never stained any of my clothing.

There is a slight odor to the wax, but it's no stronger than some of the high end waxes I have tried. I continue to came back to this wax for it's simplicity and ease of use.

Those new XL shaving heads make a huge difference. The additional blades they added change the whole feel of the shave for the better. The shave is a more friendly shave. The heads almost feel soft against your face as you're shaving. For years I have used Afta pre-electric shave to cut down on irritation but I find with this razor it's no longer needed. It is very quiet and I must agree with another reviewer that it sounds like it's on low power. It's not. I thought that the new heads would shorten the amount of time I needed to shave but that wasn't true. It took just as long. Go figure. I would have given this razor 5 stars except after I got it I realized that it does not have a low battery indicator. That was an unwelcome surprise. The 8150XL or 8151XL does.
ADDITIONAL REVIEW AFTER TWO MONTHS OF USE: Other razors wind down slowly when running out of juice. Not this one, it goes from full speed ahead to dead in three seconds. When it does that, turn it off and then back on. It seems to have another 30 seconds of shaving when you do that. I don't know if this was an accidental or intentional design but that little trick is not in the manual.

I have been using this brush now since Approximately November 1, 2010. I use it every night but not in the mornings and am totally pleased with it. Two thoughts might be helpful for those reading about problems with the water seal on the bottom of the handle and battery usage.

1)I was flabbergasted once I started using the brush by the comments of those who had trouble with the water seal. I suspect there are two types of brush users. Those like myself who keep their lips closed while brushing and therefore have no water drip down the brush whatsoever. And then I suppose there must be people who brush their teeth with their moth open and the brush handle pointed down and let water and saliva and paste drool down the handle. I suppose it would be possible to get moisture to the bottom cap of the battery compartment is you did this regularly and perhaps some moisture could get in. Or perhaps if you rinsed the brush but also rinsed the whole handle you could get moisture in.

So if when you brush your entire handle does not regularly get wet, then this complaint about the water seal will have no effect on you.

2) My experience with battery usage has been that the two AA batteries supplied with the unit lasted from 11/2/2010 until 1/16/2011 being used once a night for two minutes. Two rechargeable NiMH batteries are still going strong as of today 3/6/2011 with the same usage rate.

The brush head is still in fine condition and not splayed out.

I hope this helps folks trying to decide on this unit. Seems ridiculous to pay for a more expensive model that is virtually the same but with unneeded "features"

ADDED COMMENTS AS OF 6/5/2012: My and my wife's brushes are still going strong with no signs of fatigue, breakdown, or water seal problems. We continue to use NiMH rechargeable batteries and get months of use out of a charge. I just came back from my dentist today and they have noticed an obvious improvement in my gum health. I will be ordering another one to use in another bathroom which will be more convenient for morning brushing before leaving for work. Great product and great price savings over the unnecessary added "features" of th higher priced models!

I haven't noticed any shedding and I only needed to use 3 pieces since I was only trying to add a little length. The clips are very secure feeling in my hair & it was super easy to install myself. I am definitely a newbie to hair extensions so if you're nervous about being able to do this yourself, don't be!! I will say the hair did smell like burnt hair. If I would've known the hair wasn't a truly kinky texture, I would have gone with the regular straight. Overall I'm very happy with the product! I recommend highly.

Oh and sidenote: just for those who may be wondering. You can totally put your hair up with these clip ins. I do suggest getting a longer length though for a ponytail or high bun. No less than 14 inches in my opinion. Also if you're looking to add fullness to your hair, I'd recommend buying 2 sets. As stated earlier, I just wanted a little length in the back b/c my hair is already super thick so one set worked perfect for me.

Very pleased with this water flosser. My dentist has recommended a water flosser to me for quite some time, but I had been delaying purchasing one due to the cost. I opted for this H2ofloss water flosser for the ease of use (portability, with the small water repository) and better cost compared to other models.

PROS:
- Shipped quickly
- Multiple flosser heads for different needs
- Easy to use (charging, filling water repository, attaching different heads)
- I feel a difference, especially on my gums. Using the water flosser feels like a gum massage, which makes me feel like I'm combating gingivitis by using this.

CONS:
- Water sprays a lot, which I knew beforehand. I stick my head into the shower to use this so I don't get water everywhere.
- Water repository needs to be refilled a couple of times per flossing session (one fill lasts ~30 seconds)
- Staccato irrigation rather than steady stream. Unsure if all water flossers are like this, but it wasn't exactly what I was expecting.

Overall, really glad I finally got a water flosser, and am looking forward to feedback from my dentist.

I have searched high and low and tried handfuls of eye liners from expensive ones found in Sephora to grocery store cosmetic aisle brands.
Here's what I wanted out of an eye liner:

1. Most importantly I wanted one that would stay on my lower lid "water line" without pooling in the corners of my eyes within 4 hours.
2. An eye liner that goes on smoothly and easily
3. An eye liner that is black but that doesn't have any sparkles in it
4. One that stays all day but doesn't require a nuclear reaction and scrubbing to remove

I finally got all those things in this gel eye pencil!! It is the PERFECT eye liner for water lining. The very first day it arrived, I decided to test it out hard core. I put it on that morning at 7:30am and left on a hike in the mountains ascending 3,000ft in elevation over the course of 3 miles and back. I sweat like a pig, had sunglasses on half the time and off half the time, was wiping my face, you name it! I was absolutely in shock when I got home at 3:30pm that afternoon to look in the mirror and find that my waterline look nearly PERFECT! Unheard of. I'm so mad at myself for not taking an after picture, but I was just so tired and dirty that I forgot about it. Take my word for it though, this stuff went the distance BIG TIME!! It also came off very easily. I just put a little of my Neutrogena oil free eye makeup remover on a q tip and rubbed very gently and it came right off. WOW!

I really am blown away by this product, especially after trying so many and being disappointed. I was beginning to think there wasn't an eye liner out there that would really stay on my water line all day but I found it and I hope you found it too! I wish there was somewhere I could find this locally, but I've looked and haven't seen it anywhere yet. Thank goodness there is Amazon! And thank goodness for the people at Essence who have perfected eye liner! I can't recommend this enough. I did NOT receive this item free or at a discount

This fractionated coconut oil is a great alternative in sterilizing minor scrapes!
Fractionated coconut oil, also called “liquid coconut oil,” is a form of the oil that has had the long-chain fatty acids removed via hydrolysis and steam distillation. Just this one change makes the oil liquid at room temperature, and extends the product’s shelf life. I use this as a carrier oil to go with my essential oils and for a moisturiizer for dry skin on my arms and legs. I found another use I didn't know about. Sky Organics is full of lauric acid that is renowned for its antifungal, antiviral and antibacterial properties. This fractionated coconut oil is a great alternative in sterilizing minor scrapes, burns, and cuts. So I think I will try this application next time we have cuts or scrapes.

I've had 4 brushes now and have been wet shaving for 8 years. I was concerned that this was going to be too small in my hand or too light as I had a Parker before that was significantly heavy and had a nice sized bulb on it.

This is lighter than that, but not too light. It is smaller than others I've had but not too light. The bristles are a bit stiffer than others that I've had, but I've realised that the stiffer bristles are much better for shaving soap that I use, where the softer bristles are better for the shaving cream that I used when I first started wet shaving.

Overall, great brush. A bit of advice, when shopping for brushes, don't get caught up in all the different levels of badger hair. These are marketing people at work, and while you can tell a slight difference, softer bristles aren't always better. Rather consider - do you use soap (stiffer is better) or cream (softer is better)? Do you have a more sensitive face (softer might be better) or do you enjoy the exfoliation to keep your face soft (stiffer is better). How many passes do you take - if you only take 2 or 3, you don't need a TON of all that water holding capability that people rave about, but if you are taking 5 or more passes, that might be better for you and a bigger bulb and softer bristles might be better for you.

I was nervous about getting this. There are SO MANY models and brands and reviews out there that I finally decided on one that was within my price range to try, but also had decent reviews. I've had this now for about 4 months and have not had any re-growth of the hairs I've taken out. That's what I was looking for - lasting results. That being said, it is tricky to get used to doing this. It's best if you have someone doing it for you. There are a couple of things to keep in mind: 1. You go one hair at a time, and each one took me about 3-4 minutes, and the instructions say not to do more than a square inch of skin at a time. So depending on how much of an area you want to do, and how dense the hair is it may take months and months to clear a significant area, and that's if you do it every other day. 2.) You will have unsightly sores. You are penetrating your epidermis and electrocuting the hair follicle, so you will get a scab on each pore you zap. I used the Tea Tree lotion they recommend on Amazon and it seemed to work fine. I don't know if it would have been the same without the oil. My scabs were there the same amount of time any other scab I've had has been on me - about a week.

So it's slow going and leaves scabs, but once the scabs went away my hair has not come back at all. It's been great that way! I'm really glad I bought it. The reasons I didn't give five stars is because it said, "Clean and Easy". It's not easy, and when something leaves you scabbed over, that doesn't scream "clean" to me. The other reason is because I had to zap each follicle twice on the highest setting before it worked. But I gave it four stars because it did work! So for the price, and once you get used to it, and if you have a way to hide the scabs for a week, then it's great. I'm happy with the results, which for me is the bottom line.

I just received this dermatoscope yesterday. For the price, it is excellent. It is a simple to use, contact dermatoscope that gives the user a bright, clear image of skin lesions. I like that it is LED since those seem to last forever, and it even has a lifetime guarantee on the light source. It is comfortable to hold and the carrying case fits well in my white coat.

My reason for not giving 5 stars is that the product description claims that it can be used with or without oil. I suppose this is technically true, as I use alcohol as my immersion fluid of choice. However, if this scope is used without any immersion fluid (as implied by "oil-free epiluminescence microscopy"), it is basically just a 10x magnifying glass. The purpose of dermoscopy is to help visualize structures below the stratum corneum. If the light is not polarized (which it is not in this dermatoscope), then an immersion fluid is necessary to reduce light reflection/refraction/scatter by tiny irregularities at the skin surface in order to see the deeper structures. If you want a true oil-free dermatoscope, you will need one with polarized light (note that these are generally much more expensive and do not necessarily produce a better image). I truthfully did not expect the claim "oil-free" to hold true, but purchased it anyway since I don't mind using immersion fluid and the price cannot be beat. Therefore, I'm not disappointed, but if you are considering purchasing this mostly due to the "oil-free" claim, I'd advise against it.

Basically, if you want a dermatoscope that will give you a great image at a great price, this is your product. If you do not want to use oil or another immersion fluid, make sure you purchase a dermatoscope that is polarized.

Update: October 2013 - After using my dermatoscope for three and a half years in residency and private practice dermatology, the contact plate (where the glass contacts the skin) broke. When I contacted the manufacturer they responded quickly and even offered to send me a free replacement. I continue to be pleased with this dermatoscope and the customer service that stands behind it.

I think many guys are just buying the dye because it works well enough on its own.  But after a couple applications of the brown dye alone, I decided to buy the Organic Base just to see if it makes a difference...and it does.  When I just use the dye for 30 minutes and then wash it out, my beard/skin has that ever so slight green tint for the first 24 hrs, then a natural brown color with blondish highlights (where gray used to be) settles in nicely and lasts for about 4 days before the dye's color starts to fade away.  When I use the base first for 20 minutes, wash it out, then apply the brown dye for 20 minutes and wash it out, I don't notice any green tint at all, and the dye's color lasts about 7 days before needing another application.  So for me I'm going to continue to use the base first so I only have to dye my beard once a week to keep it looking good.  Very happy with Grizzly Mountain products.

There are two important aspects regarding this product. Price and quality...and both are positive impressions.
In terms of cost, with the current coupon, the $8 cost of this set is not just an excellent value, it's an amazing bargain. That would be a good value for just the rose quartz gua sha, alone. Even if this was a mediocre, average quality massage duo set, $8 would still represent a very good value. And, as the following review will note, the components of this set are more than worth that price.
I consider the gua sha to be the lesser of the two pieces. Still, it is a good quality rose quartz stone, with beautiful coloration. it has ample polishing, affording a shiny and smooth surface. The contour is adequately and evenly rounded with the same smooth surface as the rest of the gua sha. Close examination of the stone finds no defects or cracks, no scratches or nicks. The only concern is the gua sha is a bit thinner than I prefer. Gua sha stones seem to follow two paths: thicker with more of a rounded aspect and thinner/flattened stones that seem to be a slice of rose quartz, that's then polished to smooth out the surface and edges. This gua sha is in the latter category. It's still a valid, credible gua sha.
The highlight piece is the massage roller. This has two sizable roller heads. One is the typical smooth roller, while the other is a segmented/ribbed roller. The smooth roller is nicely polished, while the ribbed roller has more of a natural texture. On a normal dual sided massage roller, there are two different sized smooth rollers. On this tool, the rollers are distinctly different, but approximately the same size. This gives the tool a good balance.
The massage roller has the plastic grommets where the metal arm/axle enters the roller. This serves to protect the rose quartz and the metal arm from abrasive interaction. It also acts a bearing, affording a quiet and smooth rolling action. Furthermore, the plastic grommet is larger and better designed than other quality massage rollers. Another nice design feature is the metal collar/ring on the metal arm/axle at the intersection point. This design aspect is found on premium level massage rollers.
Attached are photos to show real world images of the product offering, with a ruler for size reference. Various angles and close ups are provided.
I am very impressed with the quality of design, materials and manufacture. It clearly met expectations. If the gua sha was the thicker version, this set would rate a 5+ star rating. So, if you prefer the thinner/flattened gua sha then you'll love this set. Based on my personal preferences, and overall positive impression of this set, due to the gua sha, the product receives "only' a 5- star rating. Yes, a 5 minus, just a very slight negative based on the gua sha preference. A very high level recommendation for this product.

I was a little hesitant about getting this set, since it is a little bit pricey and is a rose scented lotion (I can be sensitive to floral smells in particular). I figured I'd like the cuticle oil, and the lotion less so. But it actually turned out to be the other way around - I like the lotion better than the cuticle oil.
The lotion itself is very, very thin, almost the consistency of water, but it does work well for me. Before I used it, my knuckles were cracked to the point of bleeding. I applied a generous amount before bed, and that first night it healed my hands enough that they were no longer bleeding, just cracking a little bit. Even with my handwashing and having my hands frequently wet at work, this lotion brought them back to life. The second use resulted in no more cracks. The third brought my hands back to feeling normal. Since then, I've been using a more moderate amount only a couple of times a week (note the bottle DID come completely full - the pictures are after about three weeks of use, with the first week of that using a lot more than I normally would, due to how bad my hands were cracking). The lotion does have a definite floral scent, but it's actually a lot lighter than I expected it to be. My skin absorbs it pretty fast, within about 5-10 minutes even using a ton, and the scent fades pretty much completely even before this. So it doesn't bother me at all.
The cuticle oil, on the other hand, has a much stronger scent. It's more "planty" and less pleasant to me. Definitely not something I can use when I already have even a hint of a headache. Also, it lingers a lot more than the lotion's scent does. I do like the roller applicator as it makes it much easier to control my application onto my dominant hand, but I don't feel that this cuticle oil is anything better than any other one I've used. Combine that with the smell of it, and I definitely like the lotion in this set better. It is pricey, but to help my hands as much as it has, I think it's worth it.
