This is the best tanning lotion I have found! I love the ease of application and the color once it darkens. There are no orangy/oompa loompa undertones. A word of caution--the lotion is GREEN! I was worried I had a bad batch or something when I first opened it but it is supposed to be this way. I apply it with a black velor/velvet type mitt and I have never once had a streak. Here is the step-by-step foolproof way to get a nice, even and natural looking tan:

1. Shower and exfoliate everywhere, especially elbows, ankles, knees. I use a facial exfoliator and then I just use those exfoliator gloves for my body because if you use something with oils in it like a body scrub the lotion won't penetrate as well.
2. Dry off completely. Pull hair up/away from face. Apply regular lotion generously to feet, knees and elbows. Then start applying the tanning lotion with the mitt. I start from the legs and work my way up. Calves-->thighs/butt-->stomach/sides/what you can reach of your back-->arms-->chest-->neck-->face.
3. Run the mitt with leftover lotion on it over the tops of your feet and backs of hands.
4. Use black latex-free gloves to rub in some regular lotion over your feet/ankles until absorbed. I also apply a little regular lotion to my wrists so there is not a line. You can use the black gloves to also apply sunscreen or other face lotion if you need to.
5. Let dry completely before dressing. This lotion is awesome because it hardly rubs off on your clothes, and any that does get on clothes will wash out in the wash. Just make sure you rinse your body off (after it's been on your body for several hours and tan is set) before exercising or you'll sweat it off onto your clothes!

I started using this product as soon as I found it. It goes on quickly and easily. You just paint it on the area you want to protect from being painted and paint over it. Once the paint is dry, just peel it off.

I have used a couple of other masking products and I cannot really tell a difference between them. They all seem to work about the same. Since I use other products (MicroSet and MicroSol) I guess there is a bit of brand loyalty going on for me. Having said that, though, I would still use this even if I did not use the other products. It works and that's all that really matters.

One bottle of this will cover about a square foot or about 144 square inches of surface if you put it on thick. If you "stretch" it out a bit it can cover twice that. A 1oz bottle lasts me for several months. I recently did a 1/35 Tiger 2 from WW2 in Tiger Stripe camo. This took about 1/4 of the bottle for all the masking including the paint chipping.

If used imaginatively, this product can help you produce some pretty nice looking paint effects. I have used it to even protect areas from chipping effects with hairspray. I removed the mask before painting over the hairspray. When I went back to do the chipping, the area I covered with the mask came out with no chipping. So you can use it to protect the finish in "low traffic" areas of your model. Just use your imagination and his product will come through for you.

This is the best sunscreen for my daily skincare routine.
I have an oily & acne-prone skin and it is really hard to find a sunscreen that
- does not clog pores
- does not cause break out.
- blends well with foundation & makeup products & leaves makeup last all day long flawlessly
- does not leave white cast
- has safe ingredients suited for everyone including children and pregnant women.
- does not include artificial fragrance or paraben, oxybenzone

This sunscreen meets all the above things I’ve been looking for!!
For oily skin, I recommend using this right after a toner and then use a foundation.
There is no need to apply lotion before putting this sunscreen because it can be used as a moisturizer. Putting lotion and this sun moisturizer will be too much for the oily skin and the foundation will not blend well.

For dry or combination skin, I would recommend applying another moisturizer before applying this sunscreen before putting on foundation.

For those who do not wear a foundation, this could be used as all in one care product too! (Moisturizer & sunscreen).

I got the 22” Body Wave 13x4 T Part Lace.

I do NOT recommend this wig! I recommend you quickly exit out from looking at this one and find ANYTHING else! The hair is so fried! It looks and feels unhealthy! The knots are HUUUUGE! They look sooo unnatural! The top is thin so you can see the tracks. Since you only have 1” parting space, which is already the middle part, there’s no room to change it so you can’t even try to cover the tracks. There are stains inside the cap, which look to be from the combs. There are tons of dark hairs mixed throughout the hair (even though the product page said it was virgin hair, unprocessed hair. But this definitely did not come from a natural blonde. It was bleached and it wasn’t done well so it’s totally fried!). I would honestly give it zero stars if I could, but I have to give at least 1 star. I go into more detail below if you’re interested…

The product page states this is 150% density. I’m never good at determining if it’s accurate density. It honestly looks about 150% density. HOWEVER, there are areas on the top that are thin and you clearly see the tracks. Unfortunately, this is a T part Lace, so you don’t have room to change the part to cover the thin area. To me, that makes it unbearable.

The knots on this thing are INSANE! I have no clue what on earth is going on with them but they are HUGE!! They look so unnatural and ridiculous!

The product page says it is “premium hair material” and “we guarantee that T part Blonde 613 Lace Front Wig Human Hair Is 100% Real Blonde Human Hair Wigs, Soft & Healthy, No Tangle, No Shedding, Can Be Dyed, Bleached, Curled Etc.; You Can Buy without Worries.” Honestly, that is a joke! It is blonde, but it has been processed to be blonde. There are a ton of dark hairs mixed in throughout the hair. The hair has been badly bleached so it’s fried. I would never want to dye/bleach this again because I honestly don’t think the hair could take it since it’s in such bad condition! It definitely isn’t “healthy.” It’s not even really all that soft because you can feel how the hair is fried.

The hair they show on the mannequins in the product photos on the product page are definitely NOTHING like the hair I received! That hair looks soft, healthy, plucked well, and without humongous knots and tracks showing on top.

When you scroll down to the middle of the product page where they have more info. It says on the side in the grey box, “Wig Type: T Part Blonde Body Wave Virgin Human Hair Wigs.” It also says, “ Human Hair Quality: Unprocessed Brazilian Human Hair, Minimum Shedding.” “Just Like Your Own Human Hair, No Chemical.” Just full of LIES! This is NOT virgin hair. Virgin hair is also known as unprocessed hair (they mean the same thing). Unprocessed hair is human hair that has NEVER been chemically processed, this means over the life of the hair it’s never been dyed, coloured, or bleached. As a result, unprocessed hair is incredibly healthy. This has DEFINITELY been bleached so it is NOT Virgin/unprocessed. It is definitely NOT “no chemical.”

It has 13x6x1 T part lace. That means the lace goes 13” in the front ear to ear, it goes 6 inches back, and the parting space is 1”. The product page said, “T- part 13x6x1 Lace, Giving You the Ability to Have a Longer or Multi-direction Partings and Makes You More Natural.” If you know what 13x6x1 lace means, then you already knew when you purchased it that the parting space is only 1” wide. But, some people might not know what that 1 means at the end. They might see it, but then see the product page says “ability to have a longer or multi-direction partings” and think they can change the part if they want. Like I said earlier, you can NOT change this part! It is only 1” wide and that whole 1” is plucked out making the middle part. There’s no room to change it. It’s unfortunate because, like I said earlier, you can see the tracks next to the part and if you could change the part you’d have the possibility of covering them up. But, you can’t with this.

Oh, and there’s weird stains inside the cap. They look to be from the combs.

I thought it was really funny in the middle of the product page they had a little thing that said, “Are you satisfied with the 613 blonde lace front wig human hair you bought before? If not satisfied, you can try ours. Maybe we will become the terminator of your choice of blonde lace front wig human hair, and we hope to have the opportunity to become your superstar.” Become the terminator of your choice? What the heck does that mean?! 😂 They definitely are NOT going to become my superstar, or anyone else’s if they keep putting out wigs like this! Especially given the fact they’re charging $102.39 for the 18” body wave (at the time of writing this review) is crazy! It is nowhere near worth that! I definitely do NOT recommend!

The Maybuy Professional Hair Clippers/Beard Trimmers comes with 3 attachments, 1/2 inch, 1 inch and 1.5 inches. These attachments simply clip onto the head of the trimmer. You also get a small brush for cleaning the trimmer and a charging cable which strangely is a USB to Mini-USB even though at the bottom of the trimmer is your standard two prong charging connector.

Being able to charge this via USB however is great for when you need to go on holiday/vacation as you always have some kind of USB charging device with yourself (usually for your phone). On a full charge the clippers will roughly 2 hours and are small enough to just throw in your toiletry bag.

The trimmer itself has a nice shape to it and is comfortable to hold in your hand. It also has a fairly nice heft to it, not too light but not heavy. It feels like you are really holding on to something though.

The head is fairly wide which can be a plus in that it doesn't take long at all to trim an area but can also be a bit of a minus depending on the shape of your face if you are trimming your beard. The action is smooth and has a nice gliding feel to it, even if you don't use any attachments and just use the trimmer head itself.

It would have been nice if the Maybuy came with some sort of carrying case to put it, the attachments and the charging cord in but it doesn't unfortunately. The attachments do come in a small Ziploc bag.

Overall a nice trimmer, nice action and makes quick work whether trimming your beard or hard to reach places like around your ears.

First and foremost, this straightener requires a learning curve since it's a bit different from the traditional kind. With that being said, it's pretty cool once you figure it out! I like that it can be used with or without the steam function (and it's super easy to turn off and on). I also love the convenience of a spare water reservoir, so if I run out mid-use, I can quickly switch out a new one. Also, the little bottle that comes with this is super handy because it is marked with milliliters and makes it really easy to squirt into the reservoir. I love having 3 temperature control options (which often isn't given on other brands of straighteners), so that's a major pro. The two alligator hair clips that come with this grip my thin, fine hair really well and make it easier to section my hair for straightening. I think the carrying/storage bag for the straightener is a nice little bonus as well. The straightener gets hot super quickly and has a generous cord length. It doesn't pull/yank my hair out, either, which is awesome! All-in-all I think it's a pretty good set!

When I first used this shaver there were a few things I had to learn. It's quiet -- so quiet, I thought it wasn't working properly. Second, you don't have to press hard to get it to work -- just keep passing it over any area of your skin requiring extra attention and it will do the job. Third, the lotion/gel samples make the shave live up to its name of Cool Skin -- no razor burn whatsoever.

I have two issues with the shaver. First, Norelco still has one of the most inconvenient methods of cleaning I've ever seen. Every few days you need to disassemble the blades from the discs holding them and using a brush (I use an old toothbrush) to get the old hair out of there. Rinsing it under water doesn't do anything but remove the stray hair stubble in the head itself.

Second, the refills are difficult to find. They are relatively expensive in most sources (including here at Amazon) but are available at the world's largest retailer in a box of 5 for $4.94. I find they last about 10 days if you don't go crazy pressing the button for lotion. I also find the lotion much better than the gel. When I shave at home, I just use regular shave gel with it to preserve the expensive cartridge-based lubricants.
